maps.one_liner: How inference requests enter vLLM through the offline LLM API and OpenAI-compatible async server, routing them through async wrappers and input processors to the engine core for execution.

key_files.role: Synchronous offline API exposing generate(), chat(), embed(), and encode() methods that construct EngineArgs, instantiate LLMEngine, and batch requests through internal helpers (_run_completion, _run_chat) before engine execution.

OpenAI-compatible FastAPI server entry point that orchestrates lifespan, creates AsyncLLM engine via build_async_engine_client context manager, wires request handlers, and coordinates error handling across all OpenAI API routes.
HTTP server launcher abstraction using Uvicorn, managing socket binding, SSL certificate refresh, health watchdog task, and graceful shutdown signaling for both OpenAI and demo API servers.

Async wrapper on top of v1 EngineCore that decouples API server from engine processing via InputProcessor (PromptType → EngineCoreRequest), OutputProcessor (EngineCoreOutput → RequestOutput), and background output_handler asyncio task that multiplexes engine outputs to per-request queues.

Converts user-provided PromptType, SamplingParams, or PoolingParams into EngineCoreRequest objects, handling tokenization, multi-modal preprocessing, task routing (generation vs. pooling), and parameter validation.

Converts EngineCoreOutput batches into per-request RequestOutput objects, manages per-request output queues via RequestOutputCollector, handles logprobs decoding, beam search merging, and parent-child request tracking for n>1 sampling.

OpenAI chat completion handler that parses ChatCompletionRequest, delegates rendering to OpenAIServingRender, constructs SamplingParams with tool/reasoning parsers, calls engine_client.generate(), and streams or batches responses with delta/full format.

OpenAI completion handler that parses CompletionRequest, renders via OpenAIServingRender, invokes engine_client.generate() for each prompt, merges generators via async iterators, and formats responses with logprobs and token usage.
Base OpenAI serving class providing error handling, model validation, LoRA request resolution, tokenizer access, and beam search orchestration shared by all completion/chat handlers.

Responses API handler supporting multi-turn conversations with tool/reasoning output streaming, manages response creation, tool invocation context, harmony message parsing, and streaming event emission.
FastAPI router mounting POST /v1/chat/completions and /v1/chat/completions/batch endpoints, extracting handlers from app.state, catching errors, and returning JSON or streaming responses.
FastAPI router mounting POST /v1/completions endpoint, delegating to OpenAIServingCompletion handler, and formatting responses as JSON or event stream.

key_classes.name: LLM
key_classes.responsibility: High-level offline API exposing generate(), chat(), encode(), embed(), classify(), reward(), score() methods; orchestrates LLMEngine lifecycle, request batching, LoRA/multimodal handling, and optional progress bars via tqdm.

key_classes.name: AsyncLLM
key_classes.responsibility: Async facade over v1 EngineCore providing generate() async generator, request queueing, error propagation, output handler background task for async multiplexing, and IPC client abstraction via EngineCoreClient.

key_classes.name: InputProcessor
key_classes.responsibility: Converts PromptType (text/tokens/embeddings) and params (SamplingParams/PoolingParams) into EngineCoreRequest; validates, tokenizes, processes multimodal data, and assigns request IDs.

key_classes.name: OutputProcessor
key_classes.responsibility: Batches EngineCoreOutputs, converts to RequestOutput, pushes to per-request RequestOutputCollector queues, handles logprobs/beam-search merging, and tracks parent-child request relationships for n>1.

key_classes.name: RequestOutputCollector
key_classes.responsibility: Per-request async queue bridging output_processor (producer) to generate() caller (consumer); supports both blocking get() and non-blocking get_nowait() for low-latency draining under high throughput.

key_classes.name: OpenAIServing
key_classes.responsibility: Base class for OpenAI API handlers providing model validation, error responses, LoRA request extraction, tokenizer access, and beam search orchestration.

key_classes.name: OpenAIServingChat
key_classes.responsibility: Handles ChatCompletionRequest, renders via OpenAIServingRender, manages tool/reasoning parsers, streams or batches responses with delta format, and handles abort/cancellation.

key_classes.name: OpenAIServingCompletion
key_classes.responsibility: Handles CompletionRequest, validates beam search compatibility, renders completion prompts, invokes engine_client.generate(), merges multiple generators, and formats responses with logprobs.

key_classes.name: OpenAIServingResponses
key_classes.responsibility: Manages Responses API (multi-turn reasoning/tool use), handles response creation/retrieval/cancellation, streams response events, manages context (simple/harmony/parsable), and invokes tool servers via MCP.

key_classes.name: EngineCoreClient
key_classes.responsibility: Async IPC client abstraction providing generate(), add_request_async(), get_output_async(), abort_requests_async() methods; supports in-process or multiprocess engine backends.

entry_points: LLM.__init__() → LLMEngine.from_engine_args() creates engine for offline API
LLM.generate() → _run_completion() → _render_and_add_requests() → llm_engine.add_request() → InputProcessor.process_inputs() → engine.add_request_async() → EngineCore (process boundary)
OpenAI API server POST /v1/chat/completions → create_chat_completion() → OpenAIServingChat.create_chat_completion() → engine_client.generate(engine_input, sampling_params, request_id)
OpenAI API server POST /v1/completions → create_completion() → OpenAIServingCompletion.create_completion() → engine_client.generate(engine_input, sampling_params, request_id)

AsyncLLM.generate(prompt, sampling_params, request_id) → add_request() → InputProcessor.process_inputs() → output_handler background task pulls EngineCoreOutput and pushes to RequestOutputCollector queue
FastAPI route handlers depend on request.app.state.openai_serving_chat / openai_serving_completion / openai_serving_responses injected during app initialization
build_async_engine_client() context manager → AsyncLLM.from_vllm_config() → EngineCoreClient.make_async_mp_client() establishes IPC tunnel to engine process
data_flow: 1. User calls LLM.generate(prompts, sampling_params) or HTTP POST /v1/chat/completions
2. LLM or OpenAIServingChat preprocesses via Renderer.render_cmpl() / render_chat() → EngineInput[]
3. InputProcessor.process_inputs(EngineInput, SamplingParams) → EngineCoreRequest (tokenized, task-routed, validated)
4. AsyncLLM.add_request(request_id, request) splits into RequestOutputCollector queue + adds to OutputProcessor (current process) + sends to EngineCore via IPC (separate process)
5. EngineCore processes request batch, produces EngineCoreOutput[] with tokens, logprobs, finish_reason
6. background output_handler task: await EngineCore.get_output_async() → OutputProcessor.process_outputs(EngineCoreOutput[]) → RequestOutput → RequestOutputCollector.put(RequestOutput)
7. AsyncLLM.generate() consumer: await RequestOutputCollector.get() yields RequestOutput to caller (yield in async generator)
8. OpenAIServingChat formats RequestOutput as ChatCompletionStreamResponse, sends SSE event or returns JSON response

design_decisions.decision: Async decoupling via background output_handler task that multiplexes EngineCore outputs to per-request queues (RequestOutputCollector)
design_decisions.why: Allows API server to accept multiple concurrent requests without blocking on engine turnaround time; output_handler pulls batches of EngineCoreOutput and dispatches to per-request async queues, enabling low-latency stream_interval-based response flushing and graceful request cancellation via CancelledError.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/engine/async_llm.py:637-707 _run_output_handler() loop, L656-707 async def output_handler(); vllm/v1/engine/async_llm.py:524-636 generate() yield loop that consumes from RequestOutputCollector; vllm/v1/engine/output_processor.py:45-106 RequestOutputCollector with asyncio.Event-based blocking/non-blocking get().

design_decisions.decision: Process/IPC boundary between API server (AsyncLLM) and engine (EngineCore) via EngineCoreClient.make_async_mp_client()
design_decisions.why: Isolates API handler from GIL-constrained model execution; enables multi-rank distributed execution (TP/PP) and graceful engine restarts without crashing frontend; InputProcessor in API process handles tokenization/validation, OutputProcessor reconstructs responses without blocking engine.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/engine/async_llm.py:145-153 EngineCoreClient.make_async_mp_client() initialization; vllm/entrypoints/openai/api_server.py:108-114 build_async_engine_client_from_engine_args() context manager; vllm/v1/engine/core_client.py async protocol for add_request_async(), get_output_async().

design_decisions.decision: Continuous batching via add_request() queueing without waiting for engine turnaround; request output flushed via stream_interval scheduler config, not per-request synchronization
design_decisions.why: Enables high throughput under variable request arrival rates; scheduler batches tokens across requests (prefill + decode phases) while output_handler streams partial outputs at stream_interval cadence, decoupling request submission latency from batch formation latency.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/engine/async_llm.py:408-412 _add_request() pushes to OutputProcessor and EngineCore without awaiting response; vllm/v1/engine/output_processor.py:413+ OutputProcessor.process_outputs() merges outputs into RequestOutputCollector per stream_interval; vllm/v1/engine/async_llm.py:654 chunk_size = VLLM_V1_OUTPUT_PROC_CHUNK_SIZE batching.

design_decisions.decision: Persistent per-request state via OutputProcessor tracking across multiple output batches; RequestOutputCollector aggregates deltas for streaming or buffering for non-streaming
design_decisions.why: Avoids re-tokenization on each output batch; tracks completion status, finish_reason, usage stats, parent-child n>1 relationships, and logprobs incrementally; supports both streaming (delta aggregation) and non-streaming (full batch wait) modes.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/engine/output_processor.py:110-180 OutputProcessor state tracking; vllm/v1/engine/output_processor.py:45-96 RequestOutputCollector with aggregate=output_kind==DELTA mode; vllm/v1/engine/async_llm.py:381-398 parent request fan-out for n>1.

design_decisions.decision: Lazy output_handler startup on first add_request() call, not in AsyncLLM.__init__()
design_decisions.why: Avoids event loop requirement at constructor time (allows AsyncLLM instantiation outside async context, as needed for OpenAI server startup); defers asyncio.create_task() until first request arrives or event loop exists.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/engine/async_llm.py:170-176 lazy output_handler initialization with try/except RuntimeError; vllm/v1/engine/async_llm.py:370-373 _run_output_handler() called on first add_request(); vllm/entrypoints/openai/api_server.py:136-150 AsyncLLM instantiation before uvicorn server starts event loop.

complexity_hotspots: Async multiplexing and event loop integration: output_handler background task must avoid blocking event loop while draining EngineCore batches; chunk_size splitting (vllm/v1/engine/async_llm.py:654-683) and asyncio.sleep(0) yielding are critical for responsiveness under high throughput and concurrent request handling.

Request output aggregation and delta merging: RequestOutputCollector.put() must merge partial outputs when streaming and aggregate deltas across multiple batches (vllm/v1/engine/output_processor.py:62-76); parent-child request tracking for n>1 sampling adds complexity to output reconstruction.

IPC serialization and deserialization: EngineCoreRequest and EngineCoreOutput objects cross process boundary via pickle/serde; multimodal inputs (tensors, embeddings) require careful serialization; streaming inputs via AsyncGenerator add complexity to IPC protocol (vllm/v1/engine/async_llm.py:417-512 streaming input handling).

Tool/reasoning parser initialization and streaming state management: OpenAIServingChat integrates ParserManager, ToolParserManager, ReasoningParserManager at construction; state evolution across requests (harmony context, tool invocation, reasoning tokens) requires careful stream coordination with engine output phases (vllm/entrypoints/openai/chat_completion/serving.py:126-150 parser setup).

Error propagation and cancellation: RequestOutputCollector.put() must distinguish exceptions from normal outputs (vllm/v1/engine/output_processor.py:62-96); EngineDeadError, InputStreamError, and CancelledError require different handling paths in generate() (vllm/v1/engine/async_llm.py:598-632).

maps.one_liner: AsyncLLM is a 3-stage async facade that decouples input preprocessing, EngineCore scheduling/execution, and output postprocessing into concurrent independent loops, enabling non-blocking streaming inference via per-request async generators and event-driven ZMQ IPC.

key_files.role: Main async facade: wraps EngineCore client, orchestrates 3-stage decoupling via InputProcessor, OutputProcessor, and background output_handler loop
EngineCoreClient hierarchy: AsyncMPClient drives async input/output over ZMQ sockets; manages handshakes, output queue tasks, and async request/abort/utility calls
Data-parallel coordinator: mediates between multiple DP engine ranks and frontend clients, manages wave synchronization and request load stats
EngineCore and EngineCoreProc: inner scheduler/executor loop; runs in separate process, receives requests via input queue, produces outputs via output queue
Input stage: converts raw prompts (PromptType/EngineInput) and params into EngineCoreRequests; tokenizes, validates, handles streaming inputs
Output stage: converts EngineCoreOutputs to RequestOutput; maintains per-request state, detokenizes tokens, accumulates streaming deltas, pushes to per-request queues

key_classes.name: AsyncLLM
key_classes.responsibility: Main async facade: manages lifecycle (init, shutdown), orchestrates add_request and generate flows, spawns background output_handler asyncio task, coordinates InputProcessor and OutputProcessor

key_classes.name: AsyncMPClient
key_classes.responsibility: Async ZMQ client: sends EngineCoreRequests over input socket (add_request_async), receives EngineCoreOutputs via background task consuming output socket, manages output queue for non-blocking async iteration

key_classes.name: EngineCoreProc
key_classes.responsibility: Background process: runs busy loop consuming EngineCoreRequests from input queue, calls scheduler.schedule(), executor.execute_model(), produces EngineCoreOutputs to output queue via worker threads

key_classes.name: InputProcessor
key_classes.responsibility: Input preprocessing: validates params, tokenizes prompts, handles multi-modal features, produces EngineCoreRequest objects

key_classes.name: OutputProcessor
key_classes.responsibility: Output postprocessing and per-request queuing: consumes EngineCoreOutputs, maintains RequestState per request, detokenizes/accumulates tokens, pushes RequestOutput to per-request RequestOutputCollector queues

key_classes.name: RequestOutputCollector
key_classes.responsibility: Per-request async queue: holds RequestOutput/PoolingRequestOutput for one request, provides async get() and non-blocking get_nowait(), signals ready event when output arrives

key_classes.name: DPCoordinator
key_classes.responsibility: Data-parallel orchestration: runs in separate process, collects stats from DP engine ranks, broadcasts wave coordination messages, tracks global request load

entry_points: AsyncLLM.generate() async method: caller invokes with prompt/params, returns AsyncGenerator that yields RequestOutput until finished=True
AsyncLLM.add_request() async method: adds request to OutputProcessor and EngineCore in parallel, returns RequestOutputCollector for per-request queue
AsyncMPClient.add_request_async(): sends EngineCoreRequest via ZMQ input socket to EngineCore process
AsyncMPClient.get_output_async(): awaits on asyncio.Queue fed by background process_outputs_socket task consuming ZMQ output socket
data_flow: 1. User calls AsyncLLM.generate(prompt, sampling_params, request_id) → returns AsyncGenerator
2. generate() calls add_request(request_id, prompt, params) → InputProcessor.process_inputs() converts prompt→EngineCoreRequest (async_llm.py:L349)
3. add_request() calls AsyncMPClient.add_request_async(EngineCoreRequest) → sends via ZMQ input socket to EngineCore process (core_client.py:L1058-1061)
4. add_request() calls OutputProcessor.add_request(request, prompt, parent_req, index, queue) → creates RequestState, registers RequestOutputCollector queue (output_processor.py:L409)

5. Parallel: EngineCore process receives request, adds to scheduler, executes loop: scheduler.schedule() → executor.execute_model() → scheduler.update_from_output() → EngineCoreOutput (core.py:L402-431)
6. EngineCoreProc.process_output_sockets() thread sends EngineCoreOutputs via ZMQ output socket (core.py:L1466-1531)
7. AsyncMPClient background task process_outputs_socket() receives frames, decodes EngineCoreOutputs, puts into self.outputs_queue (core_client.py:L942-988)
8. AsyncLLM._run_output_handler() background task awaits AsyncMPClient.get_output_async(), calls OutputProcessor.process_outputs(outputs_slice, timestamp) (async_llm.py:L656-683)
9. OutputProcessor.process_outputs() updates RequestState per request, detokenizes new tokens, calls RequestOutputCollector.put(RequestOutput) (output_processor.py:L675-679)
10. generate() task loop: awaits RequestOutputCollector.get() via q.get_nowait() or await q.get(), yields RequestOutput to caller (async_llm.py:L579-586)

design_decisions.decision: 3-stage decoupling: Input preprocessing (in-process), EngineCore scheduling/execution (separate process), Output postprocessing (in-process background task)
design_decisions.why: Enables continuous batching and independent scaling: frontend can preprocess many requests while backend scheduler/executor runs in isolation, reducing synchronization points and improving throughput
design_decisions.evidence: async_llm.py:L135-143 create InputProcessor and OutputProcessor in-process; core_client.py:L146 EngineCoreClient.make_async_mp_client() launches EngineCore in background process; async_llm.py:L656-707 _run_output_handler() spawns async task

design_decisions.decision: Per-request RequestOutputCollector async queues with get_nowait() fast path: output_handler pushes RequestOutput to per-request queue, generate() pulls via get_nowait() or await get()
design_decisions.why: Minimizes event loop task switching under load (per-request isolation), supports efficient streaming of aggregated deltas, and gracefully handles client disconnects via queue close
design_decisions.evidence: output_processor.py:L45-107 RequestOutputCollector with async Event and put()/get_nowait()/get(); async_llm.py:L577-586 generate() drains get_nowait() before blocking await, avoiding unnecessary context switches

design_decisions.decision: ZMQ IPC with background input/output socket threads: AsyncMPClient sends requests over ZMQ input socket, EngineCoreProc spawns process_input_sockets() and process_output_sockets() worker threads
design_decisions.why: Enables multi-process isolation for fault tolerance, supports data-parallel deployments via ZMQ publish/subscribe coordination, and allows resource cleanup via weakref finalizers without circular references
design_decisions.evidence: core_client.py:L1001-1036 async _send_input via zmq.asyncio socket; core.py:L1320-1464 process_input_sockets() thread; core.py:L1466-1531 process_output_sockets() thread with message tracking and buffer reuse

design_decisions.decision: Lazy output_handler task initialization: _run_output_handler() creates task only on first add_request() or if already in asyncio event loop
design_decisions.why: Defers event loop dependency until needed, enabling graceful initialization before event loop exists (e.g., OpenAI server startup), and supports multiple concurrent calls to add_request without race conditions
design_decisions.evidence: async_llm.py:L171-176 try asyncio.get_running_loop() in __init__, skip if RuntimeError; async_llm.py:L637-707 _run_output_handler() checks self.output_handler is None before creating task

design_decisions.decision: Output chunking with asyncio.sleep(0) between chunks: process outputs in VLLM_V1_OUTPUT_PROC_CHUNK_SIZE batches, yielding event loop between chunks
design_decisions.why: Prevents output_handler from blocking other asyncio tasks (e.g., add_request, streaming input handlers) when processing large batches, ensuring responsive request acceptance even under high output throughput
design_decisions.evidence: async_llm.py:L671-683 split num_outputs into chunks, await asyncio.sleep(0) after each chunk, abort requests between chunks

complexity_hotspots: Multi-process IPC and serialization: EngineCoreRequest/EngineCoreOutput msgpack encoding/decoding across process boundary with tensor zero-copy via zmq.MessageTracker and buffer reuse (core.py:L1517-1531, core_client.py:L1030-1036)

Wave coordination in data-parallel (DP) mode: DPCoordinator synchronizes request waves across multiple DP engine ranks via all-reduce, manages pause/resume transitions, and coordinates START_DP_WAVE broadcasts (coordinator.py:L194-448)
Streaming input handling: _add_streaming_input_request creates async generator loop consuming StreamingInput chunks, interleaving new requests with final signal (async_llm.py:L417-501)

Request output aggregation with n>1 sampling: ParentRequest fans out n child requests, OutputProcessor merges outputs back to parent queue via RequestOutput.add(aggregate=delta) (async_llm.py:L388-398, output_processor.py:L68-72)

Graceful shutdown and circular reference avoidance: AsyncLLM uses weakref.ref in output_handler closure, BackgroundResources uses finalizer to clean up ZMQ contexts and cancel tasks without circular refs (core_client.py:L390-450)

maps.one_liner: EngineCore runs a continuous busy loop that orchestrates the scheduler and executor; EngineCoreClient/EngineCoreProc provide async IPC decoupling via ZMQ+msgpack, enabling the core to run in a separate process.

key_files.role: Main busy loop engine logic; EngineCore (in-proc) and EngineCoreProc (background proc with ZMQ); scheduler/executor orchestration; batch queue for pipelining; pause/resume/sleep semantics.
IPC client layer; InprocClient, SyncMPClient, AsyncMPClient for different execution modes; ZMQ socket management; data-parallel load balancing; elastic scaling coordination.
Zero-copy tensor IPC via torch.multiprocessing.Queue; TensorIpcSender (front-end) and TensorIpcReceiver (EngineCore); drain-and-buffer pattern for out-of-order arrival.
Message protocol definitions: EngineCoreRequest, EngineCoreOutput, EngineCoreOutputs, EngineCoreRequestType enum; FinishReason; UtilityOutput for control-plane RPCs.
SchedulerOutput: token scheduling batches, request metadata, KV cache tracking, structured output state.

key_classes.name: EngineCore
key_classes.responsibility: Core in-process loop: initializes KV cache, scheduler, executor; step() schedules+executes model; update_from_output() processes results; pause_scheduler/wake_up implement lifecycle control; manages multimodal caches and prefix caching.

key_classes.name: EngineCoreProc
key_classes.responsibility: Background process wrapper: run_busy_loop() polls input queue and steps engine; process_input_sockets() deserializes ZMQ frames (request decode via TensorIpcReceiver); process_output_sockets() encodes and sends results; handles handshakes with front-end.

key_classes.name: DPEngineCoreProc
key_classes.responsibility: Data-parallel variant: manages wave-based execution; two-phase pause protocol; all-reduce synchronization; elastic EP reconfiguration state machine.

key_classes.name: EngineCoreClient
key_classes.responsibility: Abstract IPC client interface; factory method to select InprocClient, SyncMPClient, or AsyncMPClient based on multiprocess/asyncio modes.

key_classes.name: SyncMPClient
key_classes.responsibility: Synchronous multi-process client: sends EngineCoreRequest over ZMQ input socket; receives outputs via background thread polling output socket; utility call pattern for RPC.

key_classes.name: AsyncMPClient
key_classes.responsibility: Async-await multi-process client: async send/receive via zmq.asyncio; manages output queue task; handles utility futures; coordinated stats for DP load balancing.

key_classes.name: DPLBAsyncMPClient
key_classes.responsibility: Data-parallel load-balancer: selects engine per request using P2C heuristic; routes aborts to correct engine; elastic EP scaling (scale up/down orchestration).

key_classes.name: TensorIpcSender

key_classes.name: TensorIpcReceiver
key_classes.responsibility: Out-of-band tensor receiver: drains queue, buffers tensors by sender+message, returns tensor when requested; handles out-of-order and stale tensor cleanup.

design_decisions.decision: Separate EngineCore and EngineCoreClient via ZMQ+msgpack IPC with background process
design_decisions.why: Enables async decoupling: front-end receives outputs asynchronously while EngineCore runs a continuous busy loop without yielding to event loops. Allows overlap of serialization, ZMQ IO, and model execution. Scale out front-end separately from compute.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/engine/core_client.py:460-491 (MPClient base class initializes ZMQ sockets and launches engine processes); vllm/v1/engine/core.py:1164-1171 (run_busy_loop polls input, steps engine, no event-loop integration)

design_decisions.decision: Message protocol uses byte-tagged enums (EngineCoreRequestType) for efficient socket transport
design_decisions.why: Request type can be sent as the first ZMQ frame without separate encoding; reduces overhead. msgpack encoding for request payloads (but type is raw bytes).
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/engine/__init__.py:237-250 (EngineCoreRequestType enum with byte values b'\x00' etc); vllm/v1/engine/core_client.py:802 (type_frame=request_type.value)

design_decisions.decision: Batch queue (deque with maxlen) enables pipeline parallelism by buffering SchedulerOutput+ModelRunnerOutput futures
design_decisions.why: step_with_batch_queue() schedules N batches before blocking on first completion. Reduces scheduling stalls when executor has >1 concurrent batch capacity (common with PP>1).
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/engine/core.py:188-194 (batch_queue setup); vllm/v1/engine/core.py:443-559 (step_with_batch_queue enqueues futures, returns None if queue not full, blocks only when full)

design_decisions.decision: TensorIpcSender/Receiver for zero-copy multimodal tensor transport via torch.multiprocessing.Queue
design_decisions.why: Multimodal features (images, videos) can be large; shared memory avoids pickling overhead and GPU-to-CPU-to-GPU copies. Drain-and-buffer pattern handles out-of-order arrival.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/engine/tensor_ipc.py:45-106 (TensorIpcSender calls tensor.share_memory_(), enqueues TensorIpcData); vllm/v1/engine/tensor_ipc.py:114-179 (TensorIpcReceiver drains queue, buffers by sender_id+message_id)

design_decisions.decision: Two-phase data-parallel pause protocol in DPEngineCoreProc (pending_pause + ignore_start_dp_wave)
design_decisions.why: Ensures all DP ranks pause synchronously via all-reduce. Prevents race where rank 0 pauses but rank 1 receives stale START_DP_WAVE. Enables deterministic DP execution.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/engine/core.py:1692-1708 (_pause_complete sets pending_pause=True; _has_global_unfinished_reqs calls sync_dp_state all-reduce); vllm/v1/engine/core.py:1727-1750 (resume_scheduler waits on barrier)

design_decisions.decision: Scheduler and executor are pluggable; EngineCore accepts scheduler_class and executor_class; collective_rpc dispatches to all workers
design_decisions.why: Allows different scheduling strategies (FCFS, priority) and executor backends (Ray, multiproc, uniproc) without EngineCore changes. collective_rpc unifies control plane across distributed execution.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/engine/core.py:131-153 (scheduler instantiated from config; uses SchedulerInterface ABC); vllm/v1/engine/core.py:118-120 (executor passed as executor_class); vllm/v1/engine/core.py:756-763 (collective_rpc forwards to executor)

design_decisions.decision: Async scheduling flag enables sample_tokens() to be deferred until grammar bitmask is ready (batch queue + structured output)
design_decisions.why: With structured output, sampling cannot occur until grammar tokens are available. Deferred sampling allows prefill batch to complete before blocking on sampling.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/engine/core.py:216 (async_scheduling flag); vllm/v1/engine/core.py:483-495 (pending_structured_output_tokens defers sample_tokens to next step_with_batch_queue call)

complexity_hotspots: ZMQ handshake protocol (startup_handshake, _perform_handshake): Rank 0 front-end ↔ DP Coordinator ↔ per-rank EngineCore; two-stage handshake for external/hybrid DP LB. Lines: core.py:921-1061, core_client.py:514-595.

Elastic EP scaling orchestration: 3-phase (scale-up) or 4-phase (scale-down) reconfiguration with EEPNotificationType coordination between DPLBAsyncMPClient and DPEngineCoreProc/DPMoEEngineCoreActor. Lines: core_client.py:1542-1695, core.py:1865-1977.
Batch queue + deferred sampling interaction: step_with_batch_queue() must track pending_structured_output and defer sample_tokens, then append deferred batch back to queue. Lines: core.py:443-559.

Data-parallel wave synchronization (DPEngineCoreProc): run_busy_loop polls input, all-reduces via _has_global_unfinished_reqs every 32 steps, updates engines_running flag, publishes stats. Lines: core.py:1790-1863.
Tensor IPC drain-and-buffer pattern (TensorIpcReceiver): Handle out-of-order tensor arrival, detect stale tensors, manage per-sender message IDs and tensor IDs. Lines: tensor_ipc.py:114-179.

Utility call pattern: Call IDs managed per client, futures stored in utility_results dict, matched with UtilityOutput via call_id. Handles async completion and cancellation. Lines: core_client.py:812-819, 1038-1053, 694-713.

candidate_chapters.scope: Architecture of EngineCore class, EngineCoreProc ZMQ wrapper, the request/response message protocol, and how async decoupling via background process enables non-blocking front-end I/O. Why batch queue is needed for pipeline parallelism. Multimodal tensor IPC.

candidate_chapters.title: Data Parallelism: Wave-Based Scheduling and All-Reduce Consensus
candidate_chapters.scope: DPEngineCoreProc run loop, two-phase pause protocol, all-reduce synchronization every 32 steps, wave completion signaling to coordinator. How DP load-balancing (DPLBAsyncMPClient) selects engines and routes aborts. DP Coordinator role.

candidate_chapters.title: Elastic Scaling: Adding and Removing Engine Cores at Runtime
candidate_chapters.scope: EEP (Elastic Execution Partners) scaling orchestration: 3-phase scale-up (send reconfig, launch new engines, wait for READY), 4-phase scale-down (send reconfig, stop accepting requests, wait for shutdown). ElasticEPScalingState state machine. Notification passing via UtilityOutput/call_id pattern.

9. If params.n > 1 (parallel sampling): ParentRequest(request) created; loop idx in range(n) calls parent_request.get_child_info(idx) [async_llm.py:385-400]
10. get_child_info(idx) returns child_req_id = f'{idx}_{parent_id}' and child_sampling_params (n=1, unique seed if parent seed set) [parallel_sampling.py:83-94]

design_decisions.decision: Separate InputProcessor.process_inputs() stage before EngineCoreRequest construction, decoupled from Renderer.
design_decisions.why: Enables independent testing of input validation logic, supports both sync (LLMEngine) and async (AsyncLLM) paths, allows deprecation of raw-prompt API in favor of pre-rendered EngineInput.
design_decisions.evidence: input_processor.py:L273-286 deprecation warnings; async_llm.py:L274-277 accepts both EngineCoreRequest and prompts; InputPreprocessor wraps Renderer but InputProcessor does not depend directly on render calls.

design_decisions.decision: ParentRequest fan-out: n>1 requests spawn n independent EngineCoreRequests with unique request_ids (child_req_id = f'{idx}_{parent_id}'), not a single batch.
design_decisions.why: Maintains independent scheduling per child (supports heterogeneous completion times, fine-grained preemption); each child tracked separately in scheduler; simpler aggregation logic in OutputProcessor.
design_decisions.evidence: parallel_sampling.py:L92 child_req_id format; async_llm.py:L390-398 copy(request) for each child; parallel_sampling.py:L25 child_requests set; output_processor.py parent_request aggregation.

design_decisions.decision: Multimodal features extracted and flattened into list[MultiModalFeatureSpec], sorted by token position, attached to single EngineCoreRequest (not split per modality).
design_decisions.why: Simplifies downstream scheduler integration; single consistent request boundary; position-aware ordering preserves original prompt structure for attention masking.
design_decisions.evidence: input_processor.py:L341-360 argsort_mm_positions, merge to list; MultiModalFeatureSpec contains mm_position and modality; split_enc_dec_input() preserves mm_kwargs as dict[modality] only for enc_dec case.

design_decisions.decision: Multimodal identifier includes LoRA name if enable_tower_connector_lora=True, to ensure cache isolation across LoRA variants.
design_decisions.why: LoRA tower connector embeddings are LoRA-specific; same visual input with different LoRA must generate different embeddings; prevents incorrect cache hits.
design_decisions.evidence: input_processor.py:L157-173 _get_mm_identifier() conditionally prepends lora_name; lora_config.enable_tower_connector_lora check.

design_decisions.decision: assign_request_id() generates request_id = external_req_id + '-' + random_uuid()[:8], adding entropy to prevent user-supplied ID collisions.
design_decisions.why: Supports external request ID passing while guaranteeing internal uniqueness; can be disabled via VLLM_DISABLE_REQUEST_ID_RANDOMIZATION env var (with deprecation warning).
design_decisions.evidence: input_processor.py:L215-232; async_llm.py:L386 calls assign_request_id(); external_req_id stored for output mapping.

design_decisions.decision: SamplingParams validated and cloned in process_inputs(); child requests get n=1 clone with unique seed = parent_seed + idx if parent seed is set, else cached clone reused.
design_decisions.why: Deterministic reproducibility: same parent seed -> same generation across all children; no seed -> RNG draws from system entropy, avoids seed collisions in parallel execution.
design_decisions.evidence: parallel_sampling.py:L52-81 _get_child_sampling_params() caching logic; input_processor.py:L303-320 SamplingParams.clone() and max_tokens auto-compute; sampling_params.update_from_generation_config().

design_decisions.decision: Prompt validation (length checks, vocab range) happens in InputProcessor._validate_model_input(s), not in Renderer or Engine.
design_decisions.why: Fail-fast before IPC; clear error messages to user; centralized validation logic; supports both encoder and decoder input types.
design_decisions.evidence: input_processor.py:L426-486 _validate_model_input(); L379-424 _validate_prompt_len() with model_config.max_model_len and mm_encoder_cache_size; vocab check at L461-476.

design_decisions.decision: InputProcessor has optional mm_processor_cache injection (inject_into_mm_cache) for externally pre-processed multimodal inputs, to maintain cache stats consistency.
design_decisions.why: Supports frontend decoupling: front-end preprocesses MM data and sends pre-computed mm_kwargs; backend still updates cache hit metrics for observability.
design_decisions.evidence: input_processor.py:L175-212 inject_into_mm_cache(); calls renderer.mm_processor_cache.get_and_update_item(); renderer.update_mm_cache_stats().

key_classes.responsibility: Abstract base: converts token IDs to text incrementally, evaluates stop strings against output, manages stop_buffer (holdback for excluded stop markers), num_output_tokens tracking.

key_classes.name: FastIncrementalDetokenizer
key_classes.responsibility: Uses tokenizers.DecodeStream (native prefill) for performance; handles special token spacing; error recovery for invalid UTF-8 prefix (DecodeStream reset).

key_classes.name: LogprobsProcessor
key_classes.responsibility: Assembles sample and prompt logprobs from EngineCore outputs, corrects incomplete UTF-8 sequences in byte-fallback tokens using context, maintains cumulative logprob and prompt/sample lists.

entry_points: OutputProcessor.add_request(request, prompt, parent_req, index, queue) — called by AsyncLLM._add_request() or LLMEngine.add_request() to initialize per-request state and tracking.
OutputProcessor.process_outputs(engine_core_outputs, timestamp, iteration_stats) — called by AsyncLLM output_handler or LLMEngine.step() in a loop over all EngineCoreOutput objects in a batch.
RequestOutputCollector.put(output) — called by OutputProcessor to deliver RequestOutput to per-request async queue (AsyncLLM) or aggregated return list (LLMEngine).
RequestOutputCollector.get(async) or get_nowait() — called by AsyncLLM.generate() to await or non-blockingly drain per-request outputs.
IncrementalDetokenizer.update(new_token_ids, stop_terminated) — called inside process_outputs loop to detokenize and check for stop strings.
LogprobsProcessor.update_from_output(engine_core_output) — called inside process_outputs loop to accumulate logprobs from EngineCore's new_logprobs or new_prompt_logprobs_tensors.
data_flow: 1. EngineCore → EngineCoreOutput.new_token_ids, new_logprobs, finish_reason, pooling_output (vllm/v1/engine/__init__.py:161-191)
2. AsyncLLM.output_handler or LLMEngine.step → calls OutputProcessor.process_outputs(engine_core_outputs) (vllm/v1/engine/async_llm.py:675, vllm/v1/engine/llm_engine.py:300)
3. OutputProcessor.process_outputs loops over engine_core_output in outputs (vllm/v1/engine/output_processor.py:602)
4. For each output, fetch RequestState from request_id; if none, skip (aborted request) (vllm/v1/engine/output_processor.py:603-607)
5. Call req_state.detokenizer.update(new_token_ids, stop_terminated) to detokenize and detect stop strings (vllm/v1/engine/output_processor.py:632-637)
6. Call req_state.logprobs_processor.update_from_output(output) to accumulate sample/prompt logprobs (vllm/v1/engine/output_processor.py:641)
7. Call req_state.make_request_output(new_token_ids, pooling, finish_reason, stop_reason) to assemble CompletionOutput or PoolingOutput (vllm/v1/engine/output_processor.py:644-651)
8. If stream_interval > 1, check if sufficient new tokens have accumulated; in DELTA mode, slice logprobs to delta-only range (vllm/v1/engine/output_processor.py:300-306, 394-396)
9. If req_state.queue is not None (AsyncLLM), call queue.put(request_output); otherwise (LLMEngine), append to request_outputs list (vllm/v1/engine/output_processor.py:655-660)
10. If finish_reason is not None, handle streaming_input chunks or call _finish_request() to clean up request_states, external_req_ids, parent_requests (vllm/v1/engine/output_processor.py:663-682)
11. Return OutputProcessorOutput with request_outputs and reqs_to_abort (for stop-string-triggered aborts) (vllm/v1/engine/output_processor.py:684-687)
12. AsyncLLM.output_handler puts outputs into queues; LLMEngine.step returns request_outputs list to caller (vllm/v1/engine/async_llm.py:679, vllm/v1/engine/llm_engine.py:325)
13. AsyncLLM.generate() awaits queue.get() to stream per-request outputs; RequestOutput.add(delta_output) merges if producer leads consumer (vllm/v1/engine/output_processor.py:72)

design_decisions.decision: Single-loop-per-batch architecture: OutputProcessor.process_outputs() is the ONLY function that loops over the full batch to minimize Python overhead in a continuous-batching serving loop.
design_decisions.why: vLLM v1 prioritizes serving throughput; looping N times over a batch causes N × batch_size function calls, each with interpreter overhead. Single loop amortizes this cost.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/engine/output_processor.py:590-598 (developer comment), line 602 starts single for loop over engine_core_outputs, all downstream (detokenize, logprobs, output assembly) happen within this loop.

design_decisions.decision: Decoupled async-queue architecture: RequestOutputCollector per-request queues decouple EngineCore batch output rate from per-request generate() consumer rate.
design_decisions.why: EngineCore produces batched outputs (many requests per iteration); AsyncLLM.generate() tasks consume independently. Queues allow output_handler to push ahead without blocking, clients drain asynchronously, delta-merging absorbs jitter.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/engine/output_processor.py:45-96 (RequestOutputCollector), vllm/v1/engine/async_llm.py:656-707 (output_handler loop), vllm/v1/engine/async_llm.py:576-586 (generate drains queue with get_nowait or await).

design_decisions.decision: Streaming input resumption via StreamingUpdate queue: prompt chunks applied when prior sub-request finishes, not immediately.
design_decisions.why: Allows client to stream tokens into the generator without blocking; ensures scheduler sees complete work units (prefill input chunk, then decode). Enables seamless handoff between sub-requests.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/engine/output_processor.py:116-126 (StreamingUpdate), 539-570 (_update_streaming_request_state queues or applies), 664-669 (pops and applies on finish_reason).

design_decisions.decision: Incremental detokenization with stop-buffer holdback: stop-string characters held back during streaming, released only when sequence finishes.
design_decisions.why: Prevents premature stop-string emission on partial UTF-8/token boundaries; stop_buffer_length = max(len(s) for s in stops) - 1 ensures lookback window.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/engine/detokenizer.py:84-89 (stop_buffer_length calc), 148-164 (get_next_output_text buffers or releases), 304-339 (check_stop_strings truncation logic).

design_decisions.decision: Dual detokenizer paths: FastIncrementalDetokenizer (tokenizers.DecodeStream) for HF models, SlowIncrementalDetokenizer (python-based) fallback.
design_decisions.why: Fast path uses native Rust tokenizer for performance; slow path ensures compatibility with all tokenizers. Choice auto-detected by tokenizers version and type.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/engine/detokenizer.py:22-25 (USE_FAST_DETOKENIZER version check), 60-65 (factory selection), 167-242 (FastIncrementalDetokenizer), 245-301 (SlowIncrementalDetokenizer).

design_decisions.decision: Logprobs assembled after detokenization with UTF-8 byte-fallback correction: tokenizer may split multi-byte chars; context-aware reconstruction from adjacent sampled tokens.
design_decisions.why: Byte-fallback tokenization (e.g., SentencePiece) can produce replacement U+FFFD in individual token decode; full context decoding with prior tokens recovers correct text.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/engine/logprobs.py:249-310 (_correct_decoded_token uses context chain to recover incomplete sequences), 312-346 (_verify_tokens batch-corrects), 69-119 (_update_sample_logprobs integrates detokenization).

design_decisions.decision: Parallel sampling (n > 1) implemented via ParentRequest aggregation in output layer, not in engine core.
design_decisions.why: Decouples child request scheduling (per-token tracking) from output merging (final aggregation). Allows FINAL_ONLY mode to buffer n outputs and merge only at end.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/engine/output_processor.py:269-331 (make_request_output calls parent_req.get_outputs for aggregation), vllm/v1/engine/parallel_sampling.py:100-126 (output aggregation logic).

design_decisions.decision: stream_interval parameter controls output batching: outputs emitted only after N new tokens, on first token, or on finish.
design_decisions.why: Reduces HTTP response overhead and client-side processing for latency-insensitive workloads; stream_interval=1 (default) streams every token for interactive use.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/engine/output_processor.py:285-306 (stream_interval check), vllm/v1/engine/async_llm.py:141 (config param), vllm/v1/engine/llm_engine.py:99 (init).

design_decisions.decision: RequestOutputKind (DELTA vs cumulative) handled at output assembly level: DELTA mode pops prompt_logprobs after prefill, slices token_ids/logprobs to new-only range.
design_decisions.why: Allows per-request output mode selection (streaming APIs often use DELTA for efficient delta payloads). Aggregation in RequestOutputCollector merges if producer outpaces consumer.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/engine/output_processor.py:54-56 (aggregate flag), 300-306 (delta token_ids slicing), 357-361 (pop_prompt_logprobs in DELTA), 394-396 (logprobs slicing).

complexity_hotspots: Logprobs UTF-8 byte-fallback correction (logprobs.py:249-310): multi-token context window required to disambiguate partial sequences; relies on tokenizer.decode(context+[token_id]) matching full_decoded prefix; complex string alignment fallback if tokenizer normalization mismatch.

Stop-string detection with min_tokens and stop_buffer (detokenizer.py:84-142): must avoid false positives before min_tokens emitted, hold back max(stop_len)-1 chars, truncate output_text only when certain, handle include_stop_str_in_output mode flipping.

Streaming input resumption and prefill phase transitions (output_processor.py:539-570, 664-669): req_state.is_prefilling flag must be reset on first non-prefill output; streaming_input flag drives chunk queue behavior; apply_streaming_update re-triggers prefill for next chunk; timing of input_chunk_queue state transitions (None→deque→pop) must stay in sync with EngineCore scheduling.

RequestOutputCollector merge logic in DELTA mode (output_processor.py:62-76): if producer produces output2 before consumer drains output1, output1.add(output2, aggregate=True) must correctly merge token_ids, logprobs, text WITHOUT duplication or reordering; edge case when outputs have different request indexes (n>1 child requests).

Parallel sampling aggregation (output_processor.py:321-331): parent_req.get_outputs() must track finished child requests; FINAL_ONLY mode buffers n outputs in output_aggregator by index; finished flag depends on all child_requests set being drained; interaction with streaming_input (can't aggregate mid-stream per child).

key_classes.name: Scheduler

entry_points: EngineCore.step() calls scheduler.schedule() to fetch SchedulerOutput with next batch (vllm/v1/engine/core.py:413)
EngineCore.step_async() calls scheduler.update_from_output(scheduler_output, model_runner_output) to process token generation (core.py:427)
EngineCore.add_request(request) → scheduler.add_request() to enqueue new Request in waiting queue (core.py:346)
EngineCore.abort_request(request_ids) → scheduler.finish_requests() to abort and remove from queues (core.py:354)
EngineCore calls scheduler.update_draft_token_ids() to update speculative tokens before next schedule() (core.py:441)
data_flow: 1. EngineCore calls scheduler.schedule(): step starts, kv_cache_manager.new_step_starts() resets block accounting (scheduler.py:385)

2. Schedule RUNNING requests: iterate running[] with token_budget, compute num_new_tokens from (num_tokens_with_spec + num_output_placeholders - num_computed_tokens), clamp to long_prefill_threshold (408-415)
3. Allocate KV blocks: kv_cache_manager.allocate_slots(request, num_new_tokens) returns KVCacheBlocks or None; if None, preempt lowest-priority from running, restore block pool, retry (466-508)
4. Schedule WAITING requests: similar loop, check prefix cache hits via kv_cache_manager.get_computed_blocks() or connector.get_num_new_matched_tokens() for external cache (614-641)
5. Encoder inputs: _try_schedule_encoder_inputs() checks overlap with decoder range, allocates encoder_cache_manager, updates encoder_compute_budget (695-843)
6. Construct output: _make_cached_request_data() builds CachedRequestData for already-scheduled reqs; NewRequestData for fresh reqs (872-889)
7. Return: SchedulerOutput with num_scheduled_tokens={req_id: num_tokens}, preempted_req_ids, finished_req_ids, kv_connector_metadata (910-945)
8. EngineCore.step() runs model with SchedulerOutput; calls scheduler.update_from_output(scheduler_output, model_runner_output) with sampled_token_ids (427)
9. Update phase: for each req_id in num_scheduled_tokens, append tokens to request, check stop condition, cache KV blocks, handle spec decoding rejection (1333-1378)
10. Finish: if stopped, _handle_stopped_request() re-enqueues streaming or marks finished; _free_request() triggers KVConnector async block transfers (1426-1428)
11. Return EngineCoreOutputs: dict[client_index] → EngineCoreOutputs(new_token_ids, finish_reason, events) (1520-1551)

design_decisions.decision: Token-centric scheduling with unified prefill+decode pipeline: no discrete phases, each request tracks num_computed_tokens vs num_tokens_with_spec; scheduler assigns num_new_tokens per step covering chunked prefills, prefix caching, speculative decoding uniformly
design_decisions.why: Simplifies scheduler logic, enables interleaved prefill/decode on same GPU, naturally supports prefix caching (skip already-computed blocks) and spec decoding (pad with draft tokens). Single code path handles all cases.
design_decisions.evidence: scheduler.py:352-362 'There's no decoding phase nor prefill phase'; 408-411 computes gap (num_tokens_with_spec + placeholders - computed); allocate_slots() is mode-agnostic

design_decisions.decision: Running-first scheduling with preemption: RUNNING queue processed before WAITING; if KV block alloc fails, preempt lowest-priority running request to free blocks, then retry current request
design_decisions.why: Prioritizes liveness of in-flight work, minimizes latency jitter by keeping batch warm. Preemption (zero cost if request small) faster than queuing new work. Low-priority requests absorb preemption burden.
design_decisions.evidence: scheduler.py:387-523 'First, schedule the RUNNING requests'; 479-507 preemption loop on block alloc failure; policy switches between PRIORITY (max priority) and FCFS (tail removal)

design_decisions.decision: Token budget: max_num_scheduled_tokens per step constrains batch size; running requests consume budget first, waiting requests fill remainder; allocation failure triggers preemption within same loop
design_decisions.why: Hard cap on compute work per step (GPUs have fixed forward-pass latency), predictable latency distribution, prevents runaway batches. Budget scarcity creates fair scheduling backpressure.
design_decisions.evidence: scheduler.py:371 'token_budget = self.max_num_scheduled_tokens'; 415 'num_new_tokens = min(num_new_tokens, token_budget)'; 521 'token_budget -= num_new_tokens'; 571 waiting loop checks 'token_budget > 0'

design_decisions.decision: Async scheduling with num_output_placeholders: AsyncScheduler decouples engine steps (output production) from compute steps; each schedule() increment placeholders, update_from_output() consumes them, enabling speculation without model iteration blocking
design_decisions.why: Allows overlapping spec draft generation (model output) with next prefill (model compute) on different workers. Placeholders act as future output tokens scheduled but not yet computed.
design_decisions.evidence: async_scheduler.py:32 'request.num_output_placeholders += 1 + cur_num_spec_tokens'; async_scheduler.py:52 'request.num_output_placeholders -= len(new_token_ids)'; scheduler.py:393-405 async stop condition checks placeholders vs max_tokens

design_decisions.decision: KVConnector integration: scheduler tracks async KV transfer state (WAITING_FOR_REMOTE_KVS status), connector.get_num_new_matched_tokens() queries external cache hits during schedule(), load_kv_async flag delays block allocation, finished_recving_kv_req_ids gates promotion back to WAITING
design_decisions.why: Seamless integration of distributed KV caching (e.g., inference pods, disaggregated prefill): scheduler sees remote prefix cache as extended KV store, overlaps transfers with other prefills, tolerates transfer failures (recompute policy).
design_decisions.evidence: scheduler.py:621-641 external KV query; 668-805 async load path; 2094-2136 update_from_kv_xfer_finished() drains finished_recving; _try_promote_blocked_waiting_request() L2065-2076 gates WAITING_FOR_REMOTE_KVS promotion

design_decisions.decision: Persistent batch model: scheduled_new_reqs vs scheduled_cached_reqs; new requests send full metadata (prompt, sampling_params) to workers, cached reqs send only diffs (new_block_ids, num_computed_tokens); workers cache request structs across steps
design_decisions.why: Amortizes IPC/serialization cost: large batches of recurring requests only delta-sync state. SchedulerOutput is protocol-agnostic, workers can cache locally.
design_decisions.evidence: output.py:31-65 NewRequestData.from_request() exports full state; output.py:112-177 CachedRequestData has resumed_req_ids set, all_token_ids dict (only for new steps); scheduler.py:1079-1091 skips re-sending if req was in prev_step_scheduled_req_ids

design_decisions.decision: Dual queue for blocked waiting: waiting queue (FCFS/Priority ordered) and skipped_waiting (requests blocked on async deps). Scheduler selects queue via _select_waiting_queue_for_scheduling(), re-queues skipped_waiting ahead when they unblock
design_decisions.why: Avoids head-of-line blocking: if request 1 waits for remote KV, request 2 behind it can skip and run. Blocked requests re-inserted at head when unblocked, FCFS fairness restored.
design_decisions.evidence: scheduler.py:169 'self.skipped_waiting = create_request_queue'; 582-591 check _is_blocked_waiting_status(), pop to skipped if blocked; 1567-1577 _select_waiting_queue_for_scheduling() picks skipped_waiting first if not empty; 845-846 re-prepend skipped_waiting at end of scheduling

complexity_hotspots: Preemption loop (scheduler.py:466-511): allocate_slots() may fail if memory exhausted; loop preempts lowest-priority request, restores budget, retries allocation. If preempted request == current request, break. Off-by-one risk: req_index management across preemption list mutations.

Async KV transfer state machine (scheduler.py:668-805, 2061-2093): WAITING_FOR_REMOTE_KVS status; connector async signals (finished_recving_kv_req_ids); num_computed_tokens set pre-transfer, may be adjusted if transfer fails; _update_waiting_for_remote_kv() must cache blocks after transfer. Multiple paths interact: block allocation, preemption, transfer failure.

Encoder inputs scheduling (scheduler.py:1103-1264): overlaps encoder output range [start_pos, start_pos+num_encoder_tokens) with decoder [num_computed_tokens, num_computed_tokens+num_new_tokens); if no overlap, skip; if cache/budget full, roll back num_new_tokens to before encoder input; complex boundary logic for chunked MM inputs.

Speculative decoding state (scheduler.py:525-540, async_scheduler.py:18-36): num_scheduled_spec_tokens = num_new_tokens + num_computed - num_tokens - placeholders; trimmed to scheduled list; on rejection, num_computed_tokens adjusted backward; async mode pads with placeholders; structured output grammar validates spec tokens.

Grammar bitmask generation (scheduler.py:1266-1288): filters structured_output_request_ids from num_scheduled_tokens only if not in prefill chunk; bitmask computed for decode-phase requests; integration with update_draft_token_ids_in_output() for spec token validation.

Prefix cache block accounting (scheduler.py:614-647, 744-754): get_computed_blocks() returns locally cached; connector.get_num_new_matched_tokens() queries external; num_computed_tokens = local + external; delay_cache_blocks flag for async KV load; connector_prefix_cache_stats tracks hits for telemetry.

Request lifecycle with streaming input (scheduler.py:1728-1748, 1000-1041): streaming_queue deque holds StreamingUpdate chunks; add_request() with duplicate ID queues next chunk or finishes; _update_request_as_session() splices chunks into prompt_token_ids; mm_features offsets adjusted; block_hashes recomputed.

maps.one_liner: Hierarchical KV cache block management through paged memory pools, prefix caching with block hashing, and multi-attention-type coordination for efficient token reuse and memory recycling across continuous batching.

key_files.role: Facade orchestrating allocation/free lifecycle, prefix cache hits, and coordination with scheduler; handles multi-group block management.
Low-level block allocation/eviction from doubly-linked free list; caches block hashes for prefix matching and manages LRU-like eviction ordering.
Delegates allocation to attention-type-specific managers; implements hybrid multi-attention cache-hit algorithm using fixed-point iteration.
Abstract base for attention-specific managers (full/sliding-window/MLA/etc); tracks per-request blocks, handles caching logic per attention type.
Block hashing, KVCacheBlock dataclass, FreeKVCacheBlockQueue doubly-linked list, and KV cache configuration resolution.
Metrics collection for block residency (lifetime, idle time, reuse gaps) with sampling to track cache efficiency.

key_classes.name: KVCacheManager
key_classes.responsibility: Public API for scheduler; wraps coordinator and provides `allocate_slots`, `get_computed_blocks`, `cache_blocks`, `free` methods with complex layout tracking (computed/new/lookahead/external tokens).

key_classes.name: BlockPool
key_classes.responsibility: Manages physical block lifecycle: allocation from free queue, eviction when cached, touch for reference counting, and caching block hashes in BlockHashToBlockMap.

key_classes.name: BlockHashToBlockMap
key_classes.responsibility: Hash-based lookup for prefix-cached blocks; stores KVCacheBlock or dict of blocks per hash+group_id; handles deduplication and multi-block entries.

key_classes.name: KVCacheCoordinator
key_classes.responsibility: Abstract base delegating to single-type managers; concrete subclasses UnitaryKVCacheCoordinator, HybridKVCacheCoordinator, KVCacheCoordinatorNoPrefixCache handle different model topologies.

key_classes.name: HybridKVCacheCoordinator
key_classes.responsibility: Multi-attention hybrid models; implements `find_longest_cache_hit` via fixed-point algorithm iterating over full/sliding-window/MLA/etc groups with EAGLE drop support.

key_classes.name: SingleTypeKVCacheManager
key_classes.responsibility: Abstract base for attention-specific logic: allocation, caching, cache-hit search, skipped-block handling; subclasses implement type-specific attention windows.

key_classes.name: FreeKVCacheBlockQueue
key_classes.responsibility: Doubly-linked list of free blocks enabling O(1) removal/insertion; maintains eviction order (LRU + hash-token bias); fake head/tail prevent branching.

key_classes.name: KVCacheBlock
key_classes.responsibility: Block metadata: block_id, ref_cnt, hash+group_id, prev/next pointers for linked list, is_null flag for sparse attention padding.

key_classes.name: KVCacheBlocks
key_classes.responsibility: Immutable wrapper tuple of block lists per kv_cache_group; interface between scheduler and manager to hide internal allocation details.

key_classes.name: KVCacheMetricsCollector
key_classes.responsibility: Sampled telemetry on block lifecycle (birth, access, eviction); tracks lifetime, idle time, reuse gaps for observability.

entry_points: Scheduler.__init__: creates KVCacheManager(kv_cache_config, max_model_len, ...)
Scheduler.schedule: calls kv_cache_manager.get_computed_blocks(request) then allocate_slots(...)

design_decisions.decision: Hierarchical coordinator pattern: KVCacheCoordinator abstracts concrete implementations (UnitaryKVCacheCoordinator for single group, HybridKVCacheCoordinator for multi-attention models, NoPrefixCache for disabled caching)
design_decisions.why: Enables scaling to diverse model architectures (full attention only, hybrid full+sliding-window+MLA, or mamba) without duplicating allocation logic; factory function `get_kv_cache_coordinator()` selects implementation at init time.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/core/kv_cache_coordinator.py:594-642 factory pattern; lines 324-390 unitary implementation; lines 392-592 hybrid with fixed-point algorithm

design_decisions.decision: Fixed-point iteration for hybrid multi-attention cache-hit search: iterate over attention groups, each reduces candidate hit length or accepts it; converge when length stabilizes
design_decisions.why: Hybrid models have multiple attention types with different block sizes and skipping rules (full-attention is downward-closed, sliding-window/MLA have tighter constraints). Fixed-point ensures all groups agree on cache hit length without O(product) complexity.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/core/kv_cache_coordinator.py:487-591 find_longest_cache_hit; key insight at line 521-525 'Full attn is always first if it exists' and line 575-579 convergence check

design_decisions.decision: Immutable KVCacheBlocks wrapper tuple over per-group block lists; pre-constructed empty instance to avoid GC overhead
design_decisions.why: Scheduler and KVCacheManager share block allocations across continuous batching without array copying. Immutability prevents accidental mutation. GC-optimized empty block reuse (line 158-160) reduces allocation pressure in busy loops.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/core/kv_cache_manager.py:22-103 KVCacheBlocks dataclass with overload add/get_block_ids; line 153-160 pre-constructed empty_kv_cache_blocks singleton pattern

design_decisions.decision: Doubly-linked FreeKVCacheBlockQueue with fake head/tail nodes; LRU + hash-token-bias eviction ordering
design_decisions.why: O(1) removal of any block (for prefix cache eviction), O(1) append to eviction candidates, and O(n) allocation via popleft_n. Fake head/tail eliminate branch checks in hot path. Blocks reversed on free to prioritize tail blocks (higher hash value) for eviction.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/core/kv_cache_utils.py:162-370 FreeKVCacheBlockQueue; line 162-178 design doc; line 284-302 remove and append functions use next/prev pointers without bounds checking

design_decisions.decision: Block hash computation on-demand from Request.all_token_ids via hash_block_tokens(); extra keys from multimodal features, LoRA, cache salt, prompt embeddings
design_decisions.why: Enables prefix caching across requests with same token sequence but different LoRA/MM/salt/embeddings. Hash as tree-structured data (parent_block_hash, curr_block_token_ids, extra_keys) ensures reproducibility and collision resistance for cache hit matching.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/core/kv_cache_utils.py:539-566 hash_block_tokens; 501-536 generate_block_hash_extra_keys with MM offset tracking and salt insertion at block 0

design_decisions.decision: Three-stage allocate_slots: (1) free skipped blocks, (2) handle prefix cache hits + external tokens, (3) allocate new blocks for computation + lookahead
design_decisions.why: Decouples concern of free-list management (happens even on insufficient capacity) from actual allocation. Handles prefix cache hits safely before checking free capacity, and splits token layout into overlapping regions (computed/new/lookahead) for spec-decode and KV connectors.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/core/kv_cache_manager.py:225-416 allocate_slots; lines 262-295 detailed block layout ASCII diagram; lines 300-307 three-stage algorithm description

design_decisions.decision: Lazy caching of blocks: full blocks are only cached after computation completes; num_cached_block dict tracks progress per request to avoid re-hashing
design_decisions.why: Prefix cache hits must use finalized (computed) tokens only, not draft/unverified tokens. Tracking num_cached_block avoids redundant cache_full_blocks calls on spec-decode rejection; capping at request.num_tokens (line 410-413) filters unverified drafts.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/core/kv_cache_manager.py:405-416 cache_blocks capping logic; vllm/v1/core/single_type_kv_cache_manager.py:277-301 lazy caching with num_cached_block tracking

design_decisions.decision: KV cache events (BlockStored, BlockRemoved, AllBlocksCleared) for distributed cache connectors; opt-in via enable_kv_cache_events
design_decisions.why: Enables P/D (prefill/decode) split across processes and kv-connector offloading. Events serialize block hashes and token IDs to external cache layer; metrics_collector tracks residency for observability.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/core/block_pool.py:304-320 BlockStored event construction with token_ids, lora_id, extra_keys, group_idx; line 499-509 take_events atomically drains queue

design_decisions.decision: Reference counting with touch/free_blocks: touch increments ref_cnt and removes block from free queue if ref_cnt==0; free_blocks decrements and re-enqueues when ref_cnt reaches 0
design_decisions.why: Allows one block to be referenced by multiple requests (prefix cache hit). Touch prevents eviction while block is in use. Reverse-order free (tail blocks first) biases LRU towards keeping heads, improving cache reuse.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/core/block_pool.py:391-422 touch and free_blocks; vllm/v1/core/single_type_kv_cache_manager.py:313-318 reverse free order comment

complexity_hotspots: HybridKVCacheCoordinator.find_longest_cache_hit (lines 487-591): fixed-point iteration over multiple attention groups with varying block sizes and EAGLE drop logic; convergence proof relies on monotonically decreasing candidate length bounded by 0.

allocate_slots three-stage layout (lines 225-416): complex token accounting across computed/new/lookahead/external regions with conditional sliding-window skipping and admission gating for recycling specs (SWA/chunked-local).

BlockHashToBlockMap.insert/pop (lines 75-121): transitions from single KVCacheBlock to dict[block_id, block] to handle block deduplication; pop must check and re-insert partial dict if block_id mismatch.
FreeKVCacheBlockQueue.remove (lines 284-302): O(1) removal requires maintaining doubly-linked list invariants; fake head/tail pointers must be kept consistent across all operations.

Block hashing with extra keys (generate_block_hash_extra_keys lines 501-536): multi-modal offset tracking, LoRA name inclusion, and prompt-embeddings hashing require careful state management across blocks.

Reference counting in presence of prefix cache hits (SingleTypeKVCacheManager.allocate_new_computed_blocks lines 216-218): touch must be called before extending req_blocks to prevent eviction race; num_external_computed_tokens logic intersects skipped blocks.

Recycling-aware admission cap (SingleTypeKVCacheManager.get_num_blocks_to_allocate lines 120-132): _max_admission_blocks_per_request must match pool sizer to prevent deadlock (issue #39734); per-step allocation must not apply cap while admission gate must.

maps.one_liner: Persistent GPU model runner that batches requests from the scheduler, builds input tensors via token lookup and block tables, executes the forward pass with CUDA graphs, and samples next tokens while caching state for continuous batching.

key_files.role: Core model runner: orchestrates scheduler integration, persistent batch state updates, input preparation (tokens/positions/block tables), forward execution with CUDA graphs, and token sampling with async decoupling
Persistent input batch container: maintains CPU/GPU token tensors, block table, cached request states (CachedRequestState), sampling metadata, and stateful request-to-index mapping across iterations
KV cache block table management: maps token positions to physical block IDs via slot mapping computation, supports hybrid block sizes, handles memory layout for paged attention

key_classes.name: GPUModelRunner
key_classes.responsibility: Orchestrator that cycles: _update_states() normalizes batch per SchedulerOutput (add/remove/resume requests), _prepare_inputs() builds GPU tensors from persistent batch, execute_model() runs forward+capture state, sample_tokens() samples and updates persistent batch

key_classes.name: InputBatch
key_classes.responsibility: Persistent multi-request container holding token_ids_cpu (max_reqs x max_len), block_table (per KV group), sampling state (temperature/top_p/top_k), cached CachedRequestState instances keyed by req_id; survives across scheduler iterations

key_classes.name: CachedRequestState
key_classes.responsibility: Per-request snapshot: prompt_token_ids, output_token_ids, block_ids tuple (one per KV group), num_computed_tokens, sampling_params, MRoPE/XDRoPE positions; used to populate InputBatch on add/resume

key_classes.name: BlockTable + MultiGroupBlockTable
key_classes.responsibility: BlockTable: maps block_ids to slot_mapping tensor via Triton _compute_slot_mapping_kernel (handles CP sharding, hybrid block subdivision); MultiGroupBlockTable wraps per KV-group BlockTables

key_classes.name: ExecuteModelState
key_classes.responsibility: Ephemeral NamedTuple bridge: cached after forward, unpacked in sample_tokens(); holds logits, hidden_states, spec_decode_metadata, cudagraph_stats to decouple forward/sample for async scheduling

entry_points: GPUModelRunner.execute_model(scheduler_output) -> ModelRunnerOutput | AsyncModelRunnerOutput | IntermediateTensors | None: called by gpu_worker.py after scheduler, returns None if forward not yet complete (async), caches state in self.execute_model_state

design_decisions.decision: Persistent InputBatch (continuous batching): single InputBatch object survives across scheduler iterations; add_request reuses slots via batch_update_builder instead of allocating new batch per iteration
design_decisions.why: Amortizes per-iteration state reinitialization overhead; avoids repeated allocation of token_ids_cpu (max_reqs x max_model_len), sampling tensors, block table. Critical for high-throughput continuous batching where request composition changes frequently but mostly overlaps.
design_decisions.evidence: gpu_model_runner.py:1115-1120 (NOTE on persistent batch optimization), gpu_input_batch.py:315-334 (_register_add_request reuses slots), gpu_input_batch.py:336-365 (add_request slots via pop_removed)

design_decisions.decision: Two-stage execution: execute_model() returns None and caches state in ExecuteModelState, sample_tokens() unpacks and samples asynchronously
design_decisions.why: Decouples forward and sampling for async scheduling: forward can be issued while scheduler builds next batch in parallel; eliminates GPU-CPU synchronization bottleneck at sampling. Enables pipeline overlap when PP > 1.
design_decisions.evidence: ExecuteModelState NamedTuple [gpu_model_runner.py:378-391], execute_model returns None [gpu_model_runner.py:4175], sample_tokens unpacks and comments on async [gpu_model_runner.py:4181-4213, 4390-4391]

design_decisions.decision: Block table materialized per-iteration via commit_block_table() + compute_slot_mapping() Triton kernel: maps logical positions to physical slot IDs on GPU before forward
design_decisions.why: Separates CPU bookkeeping (scheduler allocates logical block_ids) from GPU memory layout (kernel converts to slot_ids). Enables paged attention without per-token CPU lookups; Triton kernel handles CP sharding interleave logic in hardware.
design_decisions.evidence: gpu_model_runner.py:1807 (commit_block_table start of _prepare_inputs), block_table.py:141-164 (_compute_slot_mapping_kernel Triton jit), block_table.py:350-372 (CP interleave logic in kernel)

design_decisions.decision: InputBatch caches token_ids_cpu and num_computed_tokens per request; sampled tokens appended in-place, block_ids tuple swapped on request reorder
design_decisions.why: Avoids copying full token history per iteration. Since num_computed_tokens increments only on accepted tokens (not rejected drafts), same CPU tensor reused. Block IDs replaced atomically via swap_states() when requests reordered.
design_decisions.evidence: gpu_input_batch.py:131-137 (token_ids_cpu_tensor max allocation), gpu_input_batch.py:160-166 (num_computed_tokens_cpu per req), gpu_model_runner.py:3499-3501 (update in _bookkeeping_sync), gpu_input_batch.py:567-577 (swap_states reorders batch)

design_decisions.decision: CUDA graph capture and dispatch via CudagraphDispatcher: pre-capture graphs at fixed batch sizes, dispatch based on actual num_tokens/num_reqs at runtime
design_decisions.why: Amortizes JIT overhead of frequent Triton/CUDA kernel rewrites; static graphs reduce compiler variance. Dispatcher finds best-fit graph; fallback to eager if graph unavailable.
design_decisions.evidence: gpu_model_runner.py:661-670 (cudagraph_batch_sizes from config), gpu_model_runner.py:778-779 (CudagraphDispatcher init), gpu_model_runner.py:2692-2693 (dispatch call in _determine_batch_execution_and_padding), gpu_model_runner.py:3940-4000 (cudagraph_mode branching in execute_model)

complexity_hotspots: Block table slot mapping computation (block_table.py:141-164): Triton kernel must handle PCP/DCP group interleaving logic inline to compute local slot IDs for each position; subtle off-by-one errors if CP_INTERLEAVE_SIZE bounds incorrect

Persistent batch state mutation across iterations (gpu_input_batch.py:684-811 condense, swap_states, move_row): reordering batch slots requires coordinating token_ids_cpu, block_table, req_id_to_index, sampling_params in lock-step; batch_update_builder tracks added/removed slots

Async sampled token caching (gpu_model_runner.py:3456-3505): when use_async_scheduling, prev_sampled_token_ids cached on GPU to avoid CPU sync, but must coordinate with rejection sampling state machine; draft tokens may or may not be accepted

Speculative decode integration (gpu_model_runner.py:4260-4355): drafter runs after sampling but before bookkeeping; must decide which drafter variant (EAGLE/ngram GPU/CPU) based on input size; invalid draft rejections zeroed to prevent scheduler corruption

Cascade attention prefix length pre-computation (gpu_model_runner.py:1920-1922): computes prev_positions mapping to gather from prior GPU iteration's hidden states; requires tracking request ID changes and slot reorderings

maps.one_liner: Device worker lifecycle management and Executor abstraction that drives execution through multi-worker backends (multiprocessing, uniprocessing, Ray).

key_files.role: Abstract Worker interface + WorkerWrapperBase for lazy initialization with plugin support
GPU worker concrete implementation: device init, memory profiling, KV cache allocation, model loading, execution coordination with PP/TP async comms
Executor ABC: collective_rpc interface, factory pattern for backend selection, execute_model/sample_tokens delegation
Multiprocessing backend: separate processes per worker, shared memory message queues (SHM broadcast + response MQs), FutureWrapper, worker liveness monitoring
Single-process backend: thread-based worker in main process, ThreadPoolExecutor for async scheduling, direct method invocation via run_method
ModelRunnerOutput structure + AsyncModelRunnerOutput abstraction for non-blocking execution results

key_classes.name: WorkerBase
key_classes.responsibility: Abstract interface for hardware-specific workers. Defines lifecycle (init_device, load_model, execute_model, sample_tokens) and control plane API (add_lora, remove_lora, shutdown).

key_classes.name: WorkerWrapperBase
key_classes.responsibility: Lazy initialization wrapper that defers WorkerBase instantiation until init_worker is called; injects vllm_config, resolves worker class by qualified name, handles worker extensions via dynamic inheritance.

key_classes.name: Worker (GPU)
key_classes.responsibility: GPU-specific worker: manages device allocation, memory snapshots, KV cache allocation, model loading through GPUModelRunner, coordinates async PP/TP comms via AsyncIntermediateTensors.

key_classes.name: Executor
key_classes.responsibility: Abstract base for executors. Factory (get_class) dispatches to backend; collective_rpc abstracts RPC to workers; execute_model/sample_tokens return results or Futures.

key_classes.name: MultiprocExecutor
key_classes.responsibility: Multi-process executor: spawns worker processes, manages shared memory MessageQueues for broadcast and response, monitors worker liveness, non-blocking RPC via FutureWrapper.

key_classes.name: UniProcExecutor
key_classes.responsibility: Single-process executor: runs WorkerWrapperBase in main thread, ThreadPoolExecutor for async output; direct method calls via run_method.

key_classes.name: WorkerProc
key_classes.responsibility: Worker process wrapper that runs worker_busy_loop: dequeues RPC calls from broadcast MQ, invokes worker methods, enqueues results to response MQ, handles async scheduling via separate output thread.

key_classes.name: FutureWrapper
key_classes.responsibility: Future subclass that drains a deque of pending futures and aggregates responses before resolving; enables ordered lazy result collection.

key_classes.name: AsyncIntermediateTensors
key_classes.responsibility: Lazy synchronization wrapper for PP intermediate tensors: stores comm handles and postprocess callbacks, blocks on __getattribute__ until communications complete.
