WorkerProc.handle_output enqueues (SUCCESS_STATUS, output) to response MQ
Executor dequeues response from response MQ, wraps in FutureWrapper, aggregates if unique_reply_rank/kv_output_aggregator
Engine receives ModelRunnerOutput with sampled tokens, calls sample_tokens
Executor.sample_tokens -> collective_rpc('sample_tokens', ...) -> worker.sample_tokens -> model_runner.sample_tokens -> token sampling kernel -> return SamplerOutput

design_decisions.decision: Lazy worker initialization via WorkerWrapperBase: defer device allocation and model loading until init_worker is called
design_decisions.why: Enables multi-node scenarios where different processes/machines need different initialization paths; allows environment variable setup before actual worker creation; decouples config from execution.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/worker/worker_base.py:L179-L220 WorkerWrapperBase.__init__ stores rpc_rank/global_rank but NOT worker; L222 init_worker is called after environment setup.

design_decisions.decision: Executor abstraction with backend factory (multiproc/uniproc/Ray): collective_rpc is the single control plane method
design_decisions.why: Abstracts away process/thread/distributed management; engine calls execute_model uniformly regardless of backend; non_block parameter enables async scheduling without frontend changes.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/executor/abstract.py:L37-L92 Executor.get_class factory; L153-L202 collective_rpc overloads; L221-L227 execute_model wraps collective_rpc.

design_decisions.decision: Shared memory message queues (SHM broadcast + response MQs) for multi-process RPC instead of network sockets
design_decisions.why: SHM avoids serialization/network overhead for single-node multi-GPU; broadcast MQ scales to many workers without fan-out; allows collective operations on subset of workers via output_rank filtering.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/executor/multiproc_executor.py:L150 rpc_broadcast_mq = MessageQueue; L206-L219 response_mqs = [worker.worker_response_mq or peer response handles]; L373 enqueue to broadcast, L376-L395 dequeue from filtered response_mqs.

design_decisions.decision: AsyncIntermediateTensors with lazy comm synchronization: store comm handles/callbacks, sync only when tensors accessed
design_decisions.why: Pipeline parallelism needs to overlap computation with communication; lazy sync avoids blocking on irecv if tensors not immediately needed; __getattribute__ hook ensures correctness.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/worker/gpu_worker.py:L74-L103 AsyncIntermediateTensors stores comm_handles/comm_postprocess, L88-L97 wait_for_comm calls handle.wait(), L99-L102 __getattribute__ intercepts .tensors access to force sync.

design_decisions.decision: Worker liveness monitoring via background thread + death pipe sentinel for early failure detection
design_decisions.why: Uncontrolled worker crashes leave executor in bad state; monitoring thread detects sentinel closure and triggers failure callback to engine.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/executor/multiproc_executor.py:L267-L298 start_worker_monitor spawns thread listening on multiprocessing.connection.wait(sentinels), L281 sets is_failed=True if any die, L286-L290 invokes failure_callback.

design_decisions.decision: Separate async output thread in WorkerProc for async scheduling: output_queue -> async_output_busy_loop -> enqueue_output
design_decisions.why: Async scheduling needs non-blocking GPU operations; copying AsyncModelRunnerOutput to host can be CPU-intensive; dedicated thread prevents GPU stalls.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/executor/multiproc_executor.py:L622-L630 if use_async_scheduling: spawn async_output_copy_thread; L626 target=async_output_busy_loop; L936-L942 async_output_busy_loop.get() and enqueue_output.

design_decisions.decision: GPU worker memory profiling before KV cache allocation: init_device -> determine_available_memory -> initialize_from_config
design_decisions.why: Profiles actual model activation memory to leave correct amount for KV cache; avoids OOM during cache allocation by accounting for CUDA graphs, non-torch memory.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/worker/gpu_worker.py:L237-L308 init_device sets device/NCCL; L352-L504 determine_available_memory profiles with memory_profiling context, L537-L569 initialize_from_config allocates KV cache after profiling.

design_decisions.decision: Non-blocking PP tensor send via isend: return None from execute_model if sending, caller knows to skip sample_tokens
design_decisions.why: Pipeline parallelism needs to keep PP stages busy; non-blocking send allows first PP rank to return early and start next iteration while last rank is still processing.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/worker/gpu_worker.py:L773-L861 execute_model: if not last rank after isend, return None (L861); irecv uses comm_handles stored in AsyncIntermediateTensors.

design_decisions.decision: FutureWrapper with deque-based ordering: drain futures queue to maintain RPC call order
design_decisions.why: Multiprocess RPC is async but results must be drained in FIFO order to match request order; deque ensures ordered result collection without explicit ordering markers.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/executor/multiproc_executor.py:L69-L100 FutureWrapper.__init__ appends to deque; L82-L90 result() drains futures_queue.pop() until self.done(), L92-L99 _wait_for_response calls self.aggregate before set_result.

complexity_hotspots: Multi-process worker lifecycle: creation via make_worker_process, ready handshake via pipe, message queue initialization, inherited file descriptor tracking (fork-specific logic) in multiproc_executor.py:L158-L200.

Async communication in pipeline parallelism: AsyncIntermediateTensors overlaps irecv with computation, lazy sync via __getattribute__, isend non-blocking and deferred—complex to reason about. gpu_worker.py:L74-L861.

Shared memory broadcast semantics: broadcast MQ enqueues to all workers, output_rank filters responses; KVOutputAggregator aggregates multi-rank responses for KV cache connector. multiproc_executor.py:L360-L377.
Memory profiling and KV cache allocation coupling: profile_run must execute forward pass to measure activation memory, then initialize_kv_cache uses result to size paging. gpu_worker.py:L352-L569.

Error handling across process boundaries: exceptions in worker_busy_loop converted to string (not serializable), sent as FAILURE status in response MQ, executor reconstructs RuntimeError. multiproc_executor.py:L958-L970.
Workspace manager initialization for ubatching: num_ubatches depends on enable_dbo flag, passed to init_workspace_manager; affects GPU memory layout. gpu_worker.py:L310-L311.

Async scheduling: two paths (non-blocking + async output thread) for getting AsyncModelRunnerOutput, must call get_output() exactly once. uniproc/multiproc have different patterns. multiproc:L622-L942.

maps.one_liner: Attention backends and metadata: paged KV cache blocks feed into backend-specific kernels (FlashAttention/FlashInfer/Triton) via AttentionMetadata construction and backend registry/selection.

key_files.role: Core abstraction: AttentionBackend (registry interface), AttentionImpl/MLAAttentionImpl (forward signatures), AttentionMetadataBuilder (per-backend metadata construction), CommonAttentionMetadata (shared cross-layer metadata), and AttentionType (decoder/encoder variants).

Backend selection logic: get_attn_backend() resolves config (head_size, dtype, kv_cache_dtype, block_size, MLA, sparse, etc.) to a concrete backend class via platform-aware selection with KV cache layout adjustment.
Backend registry: AttentionBackendEnum (all supported backends: FLASH_ATTN, FLASHINFER, TRITON_ATTN, etc.), MambaAttentionBackendEnum, dynamic override registration, and get_class() resolver.

FlashAttention v2/v3 backend: FlashAttentionBackend (kernel support checks, KV cache shape), FlashAttentionMetadataBuilder (AOT scheduler integration, cascade/DCP support), FlashAttentionImpl (forward with varlen, quantization, DCP allreduce, soft caps).
FlashInfer backend (NVIDIA): multi-level cascade wrapper, batch decode/prefill with paged KV, TRT-LLM attention integration, FP8 dequantization, global workspace management.
Triton backend: 2D/3D unified attention kernels, cascade attention, softmax segmentation, CUDA graph support (ALWAYS), per-head quantization, slot mapping metadata.

Shared utilities: KV cache layout (NHD/HND) management, local attention virtual batching, per-layer parameter inference (window_left, logits_soft_cap, sm_scale), CommonAttentionMetadata construction helpers.

key_classes.name: AttentionBackend
key_classes.responsibility: Abstract base: defines supported_dtypes, supported_kv_cache_dtypes, kernel block size constraints, capability checks (head_size, compute_capability, attn_type), and factory methods (get_impl_cls, get_builder_cls, get_kv_cache_shape). Subclasses must implement get_name(), get_impl_cls(), get_builder_cls(), get_kv_cache_shape().

key_classes.name: AttentionMetadataBuilder
key_classes.responsibility: Generic abstract builder: constructs per-backend AttentionMetadata from CommonAttentionMetadata. Defines build() (main entry point), update_block_table() (fast block reuse), build_for_cudagraph_capture(), use_cascade_attention(), and cudagraph support flags (ALWAYS/UNIFORM_BATCH/UNIFORM_SINGLE_TOKEN_DECODE/NEVER).

key_classes.name: CommonAttentionMetadata
key_classes.responsibility: Shared metadata across all backends and layers: query_start_loc (per-request start in token array), seq_lens (cached context lengths), num_reqs/num_actual_tokens (batch size), block_table_tensor (KV block assignments), slot_mapping (intra-block token slots), encoder_seq_lens (for cross-attention), is_prefilling (prefill-vs-decode flag), positions (optional for position-dependent features).

key_classes.name: FlashAttentionBackend
key_classes.responsibility: Backend for flash-attention v2/v3: supports head_size%8==0 (max 256 for v2, 512 for v4), FA3 has full cudagraph support (ALWAYS), FA2 has uniform batch only. Implements KV cache shape (2, num_blocks, block_size, num_kv_heads, head_size) with configurable stride ordering (NHD/HND), per-head quant scales (FA3), non-causal attention, sinks, and AOT scheduler (FA3 only).

key_classes.name: FlashAttentionMetadataBuilder
key_classes.responsibility: Constructs FlashAttentionMetadata: integrates AOT scheduler (FA3), handles cascade attention (common_prefix_len split), DCP (Decode Context Parallel) with local seq lens, scheduler_metadata (tile_count_semaphore + batch vectors), max_num_splits (CUDA graph safe limits).

key_classes.name: FlashInferMetadataBuilder
key_classes.responsibility: Constructs FlashInferMetadata: manages multi-level cascade (prefix/suffix split), TRT-LLM attention plans (fast_decode_plan + batch decode/prefill wrapper generation), global workspace buffers, FP8 KV dequantization kernels for mixed-precision.

key_classes.name: TritonAttentionMetadataBuilder
key_classes.responsibility: Constructs TritonAttentionMetadata with 2D/3D kernel config: seq_threshold_3D (batch size boundary for kernel selection), softmax segmentation buffers (output, max, expsum), cascade metadata (cu_prefix_query_lens, prefix_kv_lens, suffix_kv_lens), scheduler_metadata for CUDA graphs (ALWAYS supported).

key_classes.name: AttentionImpl
key_classes.responsibility: Standard attention forward: abstract forward(layer, query, key, value, kv_cache, attn_metadata, output) returns output of shape [num_tokens, num_heads*head_size]. Manages scale (softmax scaling), kv_quant_mode (from kv_cache_dtype), can_return_lse_for_decode (for DCP), supports_quant_query_input (FP8 pre-quantization).

key_classes.name: MLAAttentionImpl
key_classes.responsibility: Multi-Head Latent Attention (DeepSeek V3): forward_mha() for prefill (full Q-KV), forward_mqa() for decode (compressed Q, cached KV_c + K_pe). Includes MLA-specific params (q_lora_rank, kv_lora_rank, qk_nope_head_dim, qk_rope_head_dim, v_head_dim) and do_kv_cache_update() (concat_and_cache_mla custom op).

entry_points: get_attn_backend(head_size, dtype, kv_cache_dtype, use_mla, has_sink, use_sparse, ...) in selector.py: selects backend based on AttentionSelectorConfig with platform-aware resolution.
AttentionBackend.get_impl_cls() -> AttentionImpl: factory returning the kernel implementation class (instantiated by model layers).

AttentionMetadataBuilder.build(common_prefix_len, common_attn_metadata, fast_build) -> SpecificMetadata: central metadata construction from CommonAttentionMetadata, called per-batch before forward pass.
AttentionImpl.forward(layer, query, key, value, kv_cache, attn_metadata, output) -> torch.Tensor: kernel invocation with Q/K/V and cached KV blocks, returns attention output.
CommonAttentionMetadata construction in gpu_model_runner.py (line 2183) and worker/ubatch_utils.py (line 233): built from scheduler output with block table and slot mapping.
data_flow: 1. Scheduler generates SchedulerOutput with request tokens, sequence lengths, block table (KV block assignments per request) -> gpu_model_runner.

2. gpu_model_runner constructs CommonAttentionMetadata(query_start_loc, seq_lens, block_table_tensor, slot_mapping, num_reqs, num_actual_tokens, max_query_len, max_seq_len) -> gpu_model_runner:2183 / ubatch_utils:233.
3. Per KV cache group, AttentionMetadataBuilder.build(common_prefix_len, common_attn_metadata) is called once per-batch across all layers of that group -> gpu_model_runner:2409.
4. Builder unpacks CommonAttentionMetadata fields: query_start_loc (cu_seqlens_q), seq_lens (seqused_k), block_table -> metadata:388 (flash_attn).

5. Builder computes backend-specific metadata: FlashAttention adds AOT scheduler_metadata + cascade split; FlashInfer adds multi-level wrapper plans + TRT-LLM dequant kernels; Triton adds 2D/3D kernel boundary + softmax buffers.
6. Returned SpecificMetadata (e.g., FlashAttentionMetadata) is cached and passed to all layers' forward() calls within that group.
7. AttentionImpl.forward(layer, Q, K, V, kv_cache, metadata, output) unpacks metadata: cu_seqlens_q, max_seqlen_q, block_table, scheduler_metadata -> forward:682 (flash_attn).
8. Forward calls backend kernel (e.g., flash_attn_varlen_func) with Q/K/V, paged KV cache, block table, and quantization scales -> forward:797.
9. Kernel maps logical block table to physical KV cache layout (NHD or HND stride order), applies paging, and computes attention with optional soft caps/sliding windows/sinks.
10. Quantized KV path: if kv_cache_dtype in (fp8, nvfp4), forward dequantizes or reinterprets cache; FlashInfer may dequant via Triton kernel; attention kernel handles FP8 scales -> forward:751-757.
11. DCP path (Decode Context Parallel): if dcp_world_size > 1, forward_with_dcp() partitions seq_lens locally, runs attention per rank, and reduces LSE across ranks -> forward:777.

design_decisions.decision: Deferred CommonAttentionMetadata -> SpecificMetadata decoupling: Builders receive common metadata and convert once per batch, not per layer, avoiding redundant recomputation across layers and enabling reuse in cached CUDA graphs.
design_decisions.why: v1 separates batch-level metadata (common, computed once) from layer-specific metadata (kernel-tuned, backend-specific). This matches async/streaming architecture where batch structure is fixed per iteration but layers may run in different waves or with different kernel implementations.
design_decisions.evidence: backend.py:516-600 (AttentionMetadataBuilder ABC with build() as single entry point); flash_attn.py:388-577 (build() computes once-per-batch scheduler_metadata, cascade logic, DCP lens reductions).

design_decisions.decision: Backend selection via AttentionSelectorConfig and platform-aware resolution: selector.py caches get_attn_backend() results by config tuple, avoiding repeated capability checks; platform.get_attn_backend_cls() is the single decider (CUDA/ROCm/CPU/XPU).
design_decisions.why: Multi-platform support (NVIDIA CUDA, AMD ROCm, Intel XPU, CPU) requires platform-specific backend choices (e.g., FlashInfer for NVIDIA, ROCm AITER for AMD). Caching by config avoids redundant imports and selection during model initialization or layer creation. Lazy import via resolve_obj_by_qualname() defers dependency resolution until actually needed.
design_decisions.evidence: selector.py:53-138 (@cache on _cached_get_attn_backend, platform.get_attn_backend_cls() call at line 114, backend.get_required_kv_cache_layout() at line 126 to auto-adjust layout after selection).

design_decisions.decision: KV cache layout abstraction (NHD vs HND stride order) decoupled from logical shape: backends declare get_kv_cache_shape() logical order and get_kv_cache_stride_order() physical permutation, selector auto-sets layout via backend.get_required_kv_cache_layout().
design_decisions.why: Different backends have different performance on different memory layouts (e.g., FlashAttention prefers NHD for coalescing; some ROCm kernels prefer HND). The abstraction lets each backend declare its best layout without forcing a global policy. Selector can override via env var VLLM_KV_CACHE_LAYOUT or KV connector's layout decision.
design_decisions.evidence: backend.py:118-147 (get_kv_cache_stride_order() docstring and usage); flash_attn.py:150-168 (cache_layout logic for NHD/HND stride permutations); selector.py:125-130 (set_kv_cache_layout after backend resolution); utils.py:48-85 (KV cache layout getter with override and env var support).

design_decisions.decision: AOT (Ahead-of-Time) scheduler integration in FlashAttention v3: builder optionally generates scheduler_metadata tensor (tile_count_semaphore + batch preparation vectors) for FA3 kernels to self-schedule within CUDA graph without CPU-side dependency.
design_decisions.why: FA3 supports in-kernel batch splitting/sorting (without per-layer CPU intervention), enabling full CUDA graph capture (ALWAYS cudagraph support). FA2 lacks this, so falls back to UNIFORM_BATCH (graph works only for uniform query lengths). This removes CPU overhead for batch reordering in FA3 prefill-decode mixed batches.
design_decisions.evidence: flash_attn.py:276-308 (cudagraph support varies by FA version); flash_attn.py:354-374 (scheduler_metadata preallocation for FA3 + CUDA graph); flash_attn.py:410-470 (build() conditionally calls get_scheduler_metadata() if aot_schedule and not fast_build and not VLLM_BATCH_INVARIANT); fa_utils.py integration (get_scheduler_metadata function).

design_decisions.decision: CommonAttentionMetadata includes optional is_prefilling, positions, seq_lens_cpu_upper_bound tensors for advanced features (spec-decode, async-spec, position-dependent models, DCP): builders ignore unset fields, enabling gradual opt-in.
design_decisions.why: vLLM evolves features incrementally. Rather than subclass per-feature, common metadata carries optional fields. Builders that need them consume; those that don't ignore. Deprecated fields (_seq_lens_cpu, _num_computed_tokens_cpu) are kept for backward compat but marked deprecated to guide migration.
design_decisions.evidence: backend.py:345-494 (CommonAttentionMetadata with many optional fields: logits_indices_padded for FastPrefillAttentionBuilder, encoder_seq_lens for cross-attention, is_prefilling, positions, seq_lens_cpu_upper_bound for async-spec, dcp_local_seq_lens for Decode Context Parallel).

complexity_hotspots: AOT scheduler metadata integration (FlashAttention v3 only): requires per-batch pre-computation of tile_count_semaphore and batch preparation vectors; breaks CUDA graph if sliding_window config varies across layers (lines 414-428 in flash_attn.py).

Cascade attention and prefix-suffix split: builders must reorder batch into prefix-only queries and suffix+prefix context KV, generating separate cu_prefix_query_lens, prefix_scheduler_metadata; forward pass branches on use_cascade flag (flash_attn.py:512-536, forward:822-850).

Decode Context Parallel (DCP) seq_lens reduction: builders partition seq_lens locally per rank using interleave_size, reducing context KV lengths; forward calls dcp_a2a_lse_reduce for log-sum-exp allreduce across ranks (flash_attn.py:482-511, forward:884-982).

Quantized KV cache with mixed layouts: FP8/NVFP4 KV caches require per-token/per-head scale factors; FlashInfer and Triton backends have custom dequant Triton kernels; FlashAttention v3 has native FP8 support (no dequant needed). Stride order and scale location matter (flashinfer.py:160-200 for Triton dequant kernel).

Soft caps and sliding windows interaction: builders must compute correct seqlens for cascade suffix when sliding_window is set; kernel behavior changes with logits_soft_cap (Grok-1 style); AOT scheduler requires constant sliding_window across layers (flash_attn.py:414-428).

maps.one_liner: Decoupled token sampling layer that processes logits through pipeline stages (penalties, logits processors, top-k/top-p filters) with configurable backends (PyTorch, FlashInfer, Triton) and persistent batch-aware state management.

key_files.role: Core sampler nn.Module orchestrating the 9-step pipeline: logprobs computation, float32 conversion, allowed tokens, bad words, penalties, temperature scaling, greedy/random sampling, logprobs gathering, and SamplerOutput generation.
Batched sampling metadata dataclass carrying per-batch tensors (temperature, top_k/p, penalties) and per-request state (generators, logits processors) to Sampler.forward().
Multi-backend top-k/top-p filter with runtime dispatch to FlashInfer (CUDA), PyTorch-native, CPU-optimized, or ROCm aiter implementations based on hardware capability detection.
Triton kernel-based top-k/top-p filtering and rejection-sampling kernels for speculative decoding with fused operations for batch efficiency.
Abstract base class and BatchUpdate dataclass defining plugin interface for request-specific logits transformations (MinP, LogitBias, MinTokens, custom).

Three builtin processors: MinPLogitsProcessor (argmax-invariant, applied post-temperature), MinTokensLogitsProcessor (non-argmax-invariant, censors EOS early), LogitBiasLogitsProcessor (per-request token bias).
BatchUpdateBuilder and LogitsProcessors containers managing persistent batch state transitions (adds/removes/moves) and classifier separating argmax-invariant vs non-invariant processors.
Wrapper over model_executor.layers.utils.apply_penalties for presence/frequency/repetition penalties; converts heterogeneous output_token_ids lists to padded tensors.
Token-sequence matching for bad words exclusion; supports prefix-matching on output history with optional speculative decoding variant (apply_bad_words_with_drafts).
Speculative decoding rejection-sampler implementing https://arxiv.org/abs/2211.17192; wraps Sampler with draft/target probability comparison and synthetic/empirical acceptance rate modes.
Per-batch reasoning token budget tracker for thinking models; applies logits mask to enforce budget constraints during sampling, with speculative decoding support.

key_classes.name: Sampler
key_classes.responsibility: Main entry point; orchestrates 9-stage sampling pipeline: raw logprobs capture, fp32 conversion, whitelist/bad words/penalties/processors/top-k-p filtering, greedy/random token selection, logprobs gathering. Delegates to TopKTopPSampler and logits processors.

key_classes.name: SamplingMetadata
key_classes.responsibility: Immutable dataclass bundling batched sampling configuration: temperature, top_k/p tensors, penalty tensors, output_token_ids lists, logits processors container, thinking budget state holder, and per-request generators.

key_classes.name: TopKTopPSampler
key_classes.responsibility: Conditional filtering module; selects backend (FlashInfer/Triton for large batches, PyTorch-native fallback, CPU-specific, ROCm aiter) at init time based on device/logprobs_mode. Outputs (sampled_tokens, optional_processed_logprobs).

key_classes.name: LogitsProcessor
key_classes.responsibility: Abstract plugin interface for per-request or per-batch logits transformations. Subclasses declare is_argmax_invariant() to control application order; implements apply() and update_state(BatchUpdate) for persistent batch tracking.

key_classes.name: MinPLogitsProcessor
key_classes.responsibility: Argmax-invariant processor censoring tokens below min_p * max_token_prob; tracks min_p count and only computes softmax when active; applied post-temperature in random-sample path only.

key_classes.name: MinTokensLogitsProcessor
key_classes.responsibility: Non-argmax-invariant processor censoring stop tokens until output length >= min_tokens; tracks requests by index; includes speculative-decoding variant apply_with_spec_decode() for masked draft token positions.

key_classes.name: BatchUpdateBuilder
key_classes.responsibility: Accumulates persistent batch mutations (removed requests, added requests, moved/swapped requests) and generates frozen BatchUpdate objects; ensures removed indices sorted descending for stable processing.

key_classes.name: RejectionSampler
key_classes.responsibility: Wraps Sampler for speculative decoding; compares draft vs target token probabilities; outputs accepted/recovered/bonus tokens; supports synthetic and empirical acceptance rate modes.

key_classes.name: ThinkingBudgetStateHolder
key_classes.responsibility: Tracks reasoning token budget per request; applies logits mask forcing reasoning_end_token when budget exhausted; integrates with speculative decoding via masked position tracking.

entry_points: ModelRunner.sample_tokens() calls Sampler.forward(logits, sampling_metadata) or RejectionSampler.forward(spec_decode_metadata, draft_probs, logits, sampling_metadata)
SamplingMetadata constructed in InputBatch with per-request SamplingParams and persistent batch state from BatchUpdateBuilder
Sampler instantiated in ModelRunner.__init__() with logprobs_mode from config; TopKTopPSampler backend selected at __init__ time
LogitsProcessors built by build_logitsprocs() in engine initialization, passed via SamplingMetadata to each sampling forward pass
RejectionSampler wraps Sampler when speculative decoding enabled in config
data_flow: 1. ModelRunner.sample_tokens() extracts hidden_states[logits_indices] → vllm/v1/worker/gpu/model_runner.py:sample()
2. Model.compute_logits(hidden_states) → [batch_size, vocab_size] tensor in float16/float32 → vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py:L68 forward()
3. If num_logprobs: compute_logprobs(logits) → raw_logprobs [batch, vocab] log_softmax → L83-88
4. logits.to(float32) → L91
5. apply_logits_processors(logits, sampling_metadata) → L93-406 applies allowed_tokens, bad_words, non_argmax_invariant processors, penalties, thinking budget → mutated logits

6. sample(logits, sampling_metadata) → L232-288 applies greedy_sample() if not all_random → potentially early return if all_greedy → apply_temperature() → argmax_invariant processors (MinP) → TopKTopPSampler(logits, k, p, generators) → select greedy vs random sampled token

7. TopKTopPSampler dispatches to backend: forward_cuda→flashinfer_sample() OR forward_native→random_sample(probs, generators) OR forward_cpu → returns (sampled_token_ids, optional processed_logprobs) → L272-277
8. gather_logprobs(raw_logprobs, num_logprobs, sampled_token_ids) → topk() + gather() + rank computation → LogprobsTensors → L122-125
9. SamplerOutput(sampled_token_ids.unsqueeze(-1), logprobs_tensors) → L136-142 returned to ModelRunner

design_decisions.decision: Persistent batch-aware state for logits processors via BatchUpdate and request index tracking, avoiding full logits processor recomputation per step.
design_decisions.why: Continuous batching in v1 requires efficient sparse updates when requests are added/removed/moved; full recomputation would be wasteful. Processor state tied to request index, not tensor position.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor/interface.py:L36-57 BatchUpdate dataclass; vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor/state.py:L18-118 BatchUpdateBuilder persistent tracking logic; vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor/builtin.py:L53-100 MinPLogitsProcessor.update_state() only recomputes tensors when min_p_count > 0.

design_decisions.decision: Separation of argmax-invariant vs non-argmax-invariant logits processors with distinct pipeline stages (non-invariant before temperature, invariant after).
design_decisions.why: Greedy sampling depends only on argmax of logits; processors that do not affect argmax (MinP, threshold-based) can be deferred post-temperature in random path only, avoiding redundant computation in greedy path and enabling early-exit optimization.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py:L245-257 all_greedy early return skips temperature/top-k-p; L268-277 argmax_invariant processors applied only in random path; vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor/builtin.py:L46-48 MinPLogitsProcessor.is_argmax_invariant()=True; L186-189 MinTokensLogitsProcessor.is_argmax_invariant()=False.

design_decisions.decision: Runtime backend dispatch in TopKTopPSampler constructor (init-time, not call-time) for top-k/top-p filtering based on device/platform/logprobs_mode, with multi-level fallback chain.
design_decisions.why: Different platforms (CUDA GPU, CPU, ROCm, TPU) have different optimal implementations; compile-time binding would require per-platform builds. Init-time dispatch avoids per-token overhead while preserving flexibility.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py:L35-104 shows dynamic selection of self.forward = forward_cuda/forward_native/forward_cpu/forward_hip based on platform checks; fallback cascade if FlashInfer unavailable; environment variable VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER override.

design_decisions.decision: Triton fused kernel for logprob gathering (compute_token_logprobs) when heterogeneous token ID lists across requests, with padding to uniform max length.
design_decisions.why: Sparse gather operations incur high memory bandwidth overhead; fused log_softmax + gather in single Triton kernel reduces round-trips and memory accesses. Padding overhead < gather speedup for batch_size > 1.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py:L145-214 gather_specific_token_logprobs() with padding logic and Triton kernel call; comment L153-159 states '~1.4x faster than sparse gather for batch_size > 1'.

design_decisions.decision: Original (pre-penalty, pre-temperature) logits preserved for topk logprobs computation, distinct from logits used for sampling (post-penalty, post-temperature).
design_decisions.why: User-facing logprobs should reflect model's true opinion before sampling-specific transformations (penalties/temperature). This differs from v0 and improves interpretability.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py:L76-79 explicit NOTE; L83-88 raw_logprobs captured before apply_logits_processors(); L98-99 if processed_logprobs returned by sampler, raw_logprobs only used if not overridden.

design_decisions.decision: In-place tensor operations (div_, masked_fill_, scatter_) throughout pipeline to reduce memory allocation overhead in high-throughput sampling.
design_decisions.why: Sampling is on the critical path; per-token latency sensitive. In-place ops reduce memory pressure and GPU allocation overhead.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py:L226 logits.div_(temp.unsqueeze(dim=1)); L383-387 masked_fill_ for bad words/allowed tokens; vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py:L299-311 in-place masking and scatter in apply_top_k_top_p_pytorch().

design_decisions.decision: Thinking budget state and speculation token handling integrated post-penalties but pre-top-k filtering, with optional thinking-token budget masking on logits.
design_decisions.why: Reasoning models need budget enforcement at sampling time; integration post-penalties ensures penalty interaction is well-defined; pre-top-k avoids confusion from double-filtering.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py:L395-405 holder.apply_to_logits() called after apply_penalties(); vllm/v1/sample/thinking_budget_state.py:L32-79 ThinkingBudgetStateHolder state tracking and logits masking logic.

complexity_hotspots: Batch update propagation logic: BatchUpdateBuilder.removed must be sorted descending; add/remove/move operations must be processed in order; persistent batch indices can shift mid-batch requiring careful index management (vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor/state.py:L59-107).

Heterogeneous logprobs gathering: padding and masking logic for variable-length token_id lists per request; Triton kernel boundary conditions; rank computation with symbolic batch dimensions to avoid torch.compile specialization (vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py:L145-342).

Multi-backend dispatch in TopKTopPSampler: fallback cascade logic with capability checks, environment variable overrides, per-request generator support limitations in FlashInfer (vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py:L30-104).

Greedy vs random sampling path selection: early-exit for all_greedy avoids penalties/temperature/top-k but requires temperature threshold check; temperature < eps comparison and tensor.where() for conditional selection (vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py:L245-288).

Speculative decoding integration: RejectionSampler compares draft and target probabilities with rejection/acceptance thresholds; synthesis of accepted/recovered/bonus tokens; spec token history integration with output_token_ids and spec_token_ids tracking (vllm/v1/sample/rejection_sampler.py:L37-100+).

Logits processor state mutation tracking: persistent batch state must be synchronized across added/removed/moved requests; index remapping on batch reordering; sparse dict state for MinTokensLogitsProcessor and LogitBiasLogitsProcessor (vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor/builtin.py:L118-332).

key_files.role: Base proposer orchestrating draft generation with multi-token lookahead, KV cache management, hidden state handling, and CUDA graph dispatch for EAGLE/DFlash
Main rejection sampling engine implementing probabilistic acceptance logic, bonus token sampling, and logprob tracking for all draft tokens

N-gram based proposer using Numba JIT to find longest suffix matches in prompt and extract following tokens without model calls

key_classes.name: RejectionSampler (v1/sample)

design_decisions.decision: Metadata-driven index indirection for logits gathering instead of explicit slicing
design_decisions.why: Enables rejection sampler to operate on flattened [num_draft_tokens] and [bonus_tokens] separately without reshaping, reducing memory allocation and GPU synchronization. Single gather operation reindexes both draft and bonus logits into common space.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/spec_decode/metadata.py:17-25 defines target_logits_indices, bonus_logits_indices, logits_indices; vllm/v1/sample/rejection_sampler.py:127-128 uses them for gather

design_decisions.decision: Triton kernels for fused slot mapping + metadata updates in multi-token loops
design_decisions.why: Eliminates launch overhead and CPU-GPU round-trips when generating sequential draft tokens. Single kernel updates positions, seq_lens, slot mapping atomically for N-token lookahead.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/spec_decode/utils.py:28-85 eagle_step_slot_mapping_metadata_kernel; llm_base_proposer.py:569 eagle_step_update_slot_mapping_and_metadata() called per draft token

design_decisions.decision: Separate CPU/GPU proposer implementations (NgramProposer vs NgramGPUKernel) with runtime selection
design_decisions.why: CPU Numba variant leverages dense token history and thread parallelism for latency-sensitive suffix matching; GPU variant minimizes host<->device sync. Caller selects based on workload characteristics.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/spec_decode/ngram_proposer.py batch_propose_numba() uses prange; ngram_proposer_gpu.py:46-150 unfold-based vectorized search; both expose propose() interface

design_decisions.decision: Persistent draft token buffers across batch iterations with rejection masking
design_decisions.why: Maintains stable CUDA graph addresses for compiled inference. Rejected tokens marked with PADDING_SLOT_ID in slot mapping to prevent KV cache writes, enabling in-place buffer reuse.
design_decisions.evidence: llm_base_proposer.py:145-147 persistent input_ids buffer; utils.py:242-271 compute_new_slot_mapping() masks rejected tokens; v1/worker/gpu/model_runner.py:182 DraftTokensHandler manages buffer

design_decisions.decision: Async proposer decoupling: draft generation before target inference in same step
design_decisions.why: Pipelined execution: while target model processes current logits, proposer can prefetch next draft tokens using previous hidden states. Reduces latency vs sequential draft->verify.
design_decisions.evidence: model_runner.py:1248 _maybe_propose_spec_decode_tokens() called after sampling, before next step; scheduler provides scheduled_spec_decode_tokens upfront

design_decisions.decision: Hidden state passing strategy: target→draft for EAGLE, target used as KV for DFlash
design_decisions.why: EAGLE expects contextual hidden states; DFlash performs cross-attention (Q=new tokens, KV=target history). Pass_hidden_states_to_model flag controls this flow.
design_decisions.evidence: llm_base_proposer.py:73, 99, 631-632 pass_hidden_states_to_model controls model() call; dflash.py:99-112 uses target_hidden_states as KV source; eagle.py:20 pass_hidden_states_to_model=True

design_decisions.decision: Two-mode rejection sampling: probabilistic (exact per-token probs) vs synthetic (pre-computed unconditional rates converted to conditional)
design_decisions.why: Probabilistic offers minimal accuracy loss but higher compute; synthetic trades accuracy for speed via lookup table. SpeculativeConfig.rejection_sample_method selects.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/worker/gpu/spec_decode/rejection_sampler.py:109-156 dispatch logic; sample/rejection_sampler.py:72-84 synthetic_conditional_rates initialization; utils.py:unconditional_to_conditional_rates()

Probabilistic rejection sampling with draft logits missing for ngram: rejection_sample() must handle draft_probs=None case by skipping draft prob comparison
Parallel drafting buffer layout: needs offset tracking for context vs query tokens, with separate position/slot mapping buffers to stabilize CUDA graph addresses

design_decisions.decision: Two-signature model initialization: new-style models accept (vllm_config, prefix) while legacy models accept (config, cache_config, quant_config, lora_config); initialize_model() auto-detects and handles both.
design_decisions.why: Enables backward compatibility with out-of-tree models while enforcing v1 contract (vllm_config, prefix) on all new models; centralizes config passing via VllmConfig.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/model_executor/model_loader/utils.py:40-96 (initialize_model signature detection); vllm/model_executor/models/llama.py:515 (LlamaForCausalLM new-style signature)

design_decisions.decision: Layer-level quantization config override: LlamaDecoderLayer.get_quant_config() allows per-layer quantization method selection; enables layer-wise online quantization without modifying model structure.
design_decisions.why: Supports emerging techniques like quantizing only certain layers or using different quantization schemes per layer; decouples quantization from model definition.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/model_executor/models/llama.py:335-337 (get_quant_config() method); vllm/model_executor/layers/quantization/base_config.py:22-25 (uses_meta_device for online quantization)

design_decisions.decision: Static forward context registration: each Attention layer registers itself in compilation_config.static_forward_context[prefix] to enable torch.compile introspection; used for KV sharing validation and batch invariance.
design_decisions.why: v1 design requires torch.compile to understand layer relationships; static context allows custom passes (KV sharing, batch invariance) without modifying forward signature.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/model_executor/layers/attention/attention.py:367-370 (register in static_forward_context); vllm/v1/attention/backend.py (AttentionBackend interface)

design_decisions.decision: Stacked parameter mapping for QKV/gate-up projection: LlamaModel.load_weights() maps separate q_proj/k_proj/v_proj from checkpoint to fused qkv_proj parameter; reverse mapping for gate_proj/up_proj.
design_decisions.why: HuggingFace checkpoints store unfused projections; vLLM fuses for better hardware utilization; mapping layer decouples checkpoint format from runtime format.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/model_executor/models/llama.py:437-444 (stacked_params_mapping); vllm/model_executor/layers/linear.py:114-138 (adjust_scalar_to_fused_array for shard loading)

design_decisions.decision: AutoWeightsLoader with recursive module grouping: weights are grouped by prefix and dispatched to submodules; submodules can override load_weights() to customize (e.g., LoRA); reduces checkpoint iteration to one pass.
design_decisions.why: Single checkpoint pass is I/O critical; recursive dispatch avoids reimplementing weight loading in every model; enables LoRA/adapter models to intercept weights.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/model_executor/models/utils.py:117-230 (AutoWeightsLoader._groupby_prefix); vllm/model_executor/models/llama.py:584-589 (LlamaForCausalLM delegates to AutoWeightsLoader)

design_decisions.decision: Backend-agnostic attention: Attention class delegates to pluggable AttentionBackend (FlashInfer, Triton, etc.); backend selected at init time based on dtype/kv_cache_dtype/device.
design_decisions.why: Enables new kernels (FlashInfer 0.9, FBGEMM, etc.) without modifying Attention; supports per-model backend selection; KV cache layout abstraction via get_kv_cache_shape().
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/model_executor/layers/attention/attention.py:298-310 (get_attn_backend selector); vllm/v1/attention/backend.py:68-96 (get_kv_cache_shape abstraction)

design_decisions.decision: Position embedding decoupling: rotary embeddings created via get_rope() factory and applied in LlamaAttention.forward() AFTER qkv_proj; supports 10+ RoPE variants without model changes.
design_decisions.why: RoPE scaling strategies vary by model (Llama3, DeepSeek scaling, NTK, YaRN); factory caches instances by rope_parameters config; allows composition with other position methods.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/model_executor/layers/rotary_embedding/__init__.py:33-170 (get_rope factory and variants); vllm/model_executor/models/llama.py:229-230 (position embedding apply in forward)

design_decisions.decision: Weight post-processing separation: weight_loader callbacks are applied during checkpoint iteration; quant_method.process_weights_after_loading() runs AFTER all weights loaded (e.g., for repack/transpose).
design_decisions.why: Separates I/O (streaming weights) from compute (repacking); enables online quantization with meta-device weights; allows kernels (Marlin, CUTLASS) to operate on final weight format.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/model_executor/model_loader/utils.py:99-122 (device_loading_context for post-load); vllm/model_executor/layers/quantization/base_config.py:50-55 (process_weights_after_loading interface)

complexity_hotspots: Tensor parallelism integration in linear layers: ColumnParallelLinear/RowParallelLinear dispatch to distributed primitives (split_tensor_along_last_dim, allreduce) and integrate quantization apply(); requires coordination of weight sharding and allgather/allreduce semantics.

QKV/gate-up stacking and sharding: LlamaModel.load_weights() maps checkpoint projections to fused parameters; shard_id routing in weight_loader() handles per-head KV replication vs. partitioning logic and scalar-to-fused-array adjustments.

Backend-specific KV cache layouts: AttentionBackend.get_kv_cache_shape() and get_kv_cache_stride_order() abstract memory layout; FlashInfer/Triton differ in block indexing, stride ordering, and quantization scale placement; Attention bridges these via impl_cls.

Quantization method dispatch and weight creation: QuantizationConfig.get_quant_method() returns method-specific implementations; create_weights() must register buffers (scales) in state dict for model.to(device) to move them; post-load processing (online quantization) complicates memory flow.

Rotary embedding variant selection: rope_parameters dict controls scaling strategy, dimension, base; get_rope() caches by tuple key; DeepSeekScalingRotaryEmbedding and Llama3RotaryEmbedding override base implementation; dual_chunk_rope for attention variants adds orthogonal complexity.

Pipeline parallelism with layer skipping: make_layers() computes start_layer/end_layer for each rank; PPMissingLayer() placeholders replace embed_tokens/norm on non-first/non-last ranks; intermediate_tensors IntermediateTensors carrier protocol bridges layer boundaries.

Weight loading with online quantization: meta-device weight creation (uses_meta_device=True) defers allocation; process_weights_after_loading() must move to target_device then back to CPU for pin_memory; UVA offloading re-applies after processing.

Static forward context for torch.compile: Attention registers itself by prefix string in global compilation_config.static_forward_context; validate_kv_sharing_target() inspects this dict to resolve layer dependencies; batch invariance optimizations read registered attn_type and sliding_window.

maps.one_liner: Manages multi-GPU/multi-node model execution through distributed process groups, collective communication primitives (all-reduce, all-gather, reduce-scatter), and backend-agnostic tensor/pipeline/data parallelism sharding across multiprocess and Ray executors.

key_files.role: Core distributed state management: GroupCoordinator class, parallel group initialization (TP, PP, DP, PCP, DCP, EP), collective operations (all_reduce, all_gather, reduce_scatter), and torch.distributed ProcessGroup wrapper with custom op registration.

Multiprocessing executor: spawns worker processes per GPU, manages IPC via MessageQueue (shared memory + ZMQ), coordinates collective RPC to workers via broadcast/response queues, implements async scheduling with futures.

Ray-based distributed executor: actor-based workers, compiled DAG for efficient distributed forward pass, collective RPC via actor method calls, placement group scheduling for topology-aware placement.
Abstract device communicator interface: all_reduce, all_gather, reduce_scatter, gather, send/recv operations with optional device-specific backends (NCCL, custom AllReduce, CPU communicator).
Shared memory broadcast infrastructure: MessageQueue with ring buffer for lock-free IPC, SpinCondition for producer-consumer synchronization, ZMQ for inter-node communication.
Public API for collective operations: tensor_model_parallel_all_reduce, all_gather, reduce_scatter, gather, broadcast_tensor_dict—thin wrappers around GroupCoordinator.
Executor base class: factory pattern for executor selection (mp/ray/uni), abstract collective_rpc interface, overloaded execute_model/sample_tokens with non_block futures.
Worker base class: interface for device-specific workers, defers init_device/load_model/execute_model to subclasses, receives rank, distributed_init_method, parallel config.
CUDA-specific communicator: NCCL backend, graph capture for inference, custom all-reduce kernels, ca_comm integration for optimized reductions.

key_classes.name: GroupCoordinator
key_classes.responsibility: PyTorch ProcessGroup wrapper; manages one communication group (TP, PP, DP, etc.) with both CPU (gloo) and device (NCCL) groups; exposes all_reduce, all_gather, reduce_scatter, gather, broadcast, send/recv with optional device communicator backend.

key_classes.name: MultiprocExecutor
key_classes.responsibility: Spawns per-GPU worker processes, owns rpc_broadcast_mq (input) and response_mqs (output), orchestrates collective RPC via FutureWrapper with ordered draining, monitors worker health.

key_classes.name: WorkerProc
key_classes.responsibility: Runs in worker process: initializes parallel groups, loads model, runs worker_busy_loop() dequeuing RPC methods from rpc_broadcast_mq, handles output via enqueue_output/handle_output, supports async scheduling.

key_classes.name: RayDistributedExecutor
key_classes.responsibility: Manages Ray actors as distributed workers, compiles forward DAG with pp_tp_workers topology, implements collective_rpc via ray.remote method calls, supports Ray's compiled DAG channel (NCCL/SHM).

key_classes.name: DeviceCommunicatorBase
key_classes.responsibility: Abstracts device-specific collective ops (all_reduce, all_gather, reduce_scatter, gather, send/recv); dispatches to torch.distributed or custom backends; handles stateless and stateful process groups.

key_classes.name: MessageQueue
key_classes.responsibility: Lock-free shared memory IPC: ring buffer for pickled messages, SpinCondition producer-consumer sync, ZMQ for inter-node broadcast, export_handle/import_handle for distributed instantiation.

key_classes.name: Executor (abstract)
key_classes.responsibility: Factory pattern (get_class), abstract collective_rpc, wraps execute_model/sample_tokens to extract rank-0 output, manages failure callbacks and async scheduling flags.

vllm/distributed/parallel_state.py:initialize_model_parallel() — Reorganizes world into TP/PP/DP/PCP/DCP/EP groups post torch.distributed init; builds group_ranks tensors and calls init_model_parallel_group for each.

vllm/v1/executor/multiproc_executor.py:WorkerProc.worker_main() — Static worker entry point: initializes parallel state, loads model, runs worker_busy_loop() indefinitely.
vllm/v1/executor/ray_executor.py:RayDistributedExecutor._init_executor() — Creates Ray actors, compiles forward DAG, initializes workers via collective RPC.

data_flow: User calls LLM.generate() → vllm/v1/engine/async_llm.py:AsyncLLM._run_step() → engine_core.step()
engine_core.step() → Scheduler returns SchedulerOutput (token_ids, batches)
executor.execute_model(scheduler_output, non_block=True) — returns Future/ModelRunnerOutput
MultiprocExecutor.collective_rpc('execute_model', args=(scheduler_output,)) — enqueues to rpc_broadcast_mq shared memory ring buffer
WorkerProc.worker_busy_loop() dequeues (method, args, kwargs, output_rank) from rpc_broadcast_mq
WorkerProc invokes self.worker.execute_model(scheduler_output) — calls ModelRunner.forward()
ModelRunner.forward() may call vllm/distributed/communication_op.py:tensor_model_parallel_all_reduce(tensor) for cross-TP communication
tensor_model_parallel_all_reduce() → get_tp_group().all_reduce(tensor) → GroupCoordinator.all_reduce()
GroupCoordinator delegates to DeviceCommunicator.all_reduce() (CUDA/CPU/custom backend)
DeviceCommunicator.all_reduce() calls torch.distributed.all_reduce(tensor, group=device_group) [NCCL backend]
NCCL performs collective across TP group, model output aggregated to tp_rank=0
ModelRunnerOutput enqueued to worker_response_mq (shared memory broadcast) by WorkerProc.handle_output()
MultiprocExecutor.collective_rpc dequeues responses from response_mqs (indexed by output_rank per PP stage)
Future.result() returns aggregated output → engine continues to sampling, scheduler next iteration
RayDistributedExecutor flow similar but uses ray.remote for method calls and ray.get() for blocking; compiled DAG executes forward + PP communication in single graph.

design_decisions.decision: Lock-free shared memory IPC (MessageQueue + ring buffer) for intra-node multiprocess executor instead of serialization over network sockets.
design_decisions.why: Reduces latency and CPU overhead for control-plane RPC in tight training/inference loops; avoids pickling serialization cost for frequent SchedulerOutput messages.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/distributed/device_communicators/shm_broadcast.py:MessageQueue (L1-917), SpinCondition (L92-150), memory_fence() (L55-73); vllm/v1/executor/multiproc_executor.py:MultiprocExecutor._init_executor L150-156 (creates rpc_broadcast_mq), WorkerProc.worker_busy_loop L944-970 (dequeues from rpc_broadcast_mq).

design_decisions.decision: Separate CPU (gloo) and device (NCCL/custom) process groups within each GroupCoordinator for hybrid collective ops; metadata exchange via CPU, tensor ops via device.
design_decisions.why: Decouples control-plane communication (ProcessGroup ranking, metadata broadcasts) from data-plane (NCCL all-reduce); allows CPU tensors to use gloo, GPU tensors to use NCCL transparently.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/distributed/parallel_state.py:GroupCoordinator.__init__ L319-381 (creates cpu_group with gloo, device_group with NCCL backend); broadcast_tensor_dict L725-809 uses cpu_group for metadata, device_group for tensors.

design_decisions.decision: Ray compiled DAG over standard Ray remote calls for forward pass to enable zero-copy tensor passing and fused PP+TP communication.
design_decisions.why: Reduces serialization overhead and inter-process communication latency in pipeline parallelism; compiled DAG JIT-compiles communication topology at graph level.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/executor/ray_executor.py:_init_executor L70-87 (initializes forward_dag), _execute_dag L451-485 (calls self.forward_dag.execute), _compiled_ray_dag L544+ (builds InputNode/MultiOutputNode DAG structure).

design_decisions.decision: Async scheduling decoupled from distributed orchestration: non_block=True futures allow engine to enqueue next batch while current batch communicates, with optional aggregation post-execution.
design_decisions.why: Hides collective communication latency behind scheduler, enables pipelining of multiple batches across workers without blocking main async loop.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/executor/abstract.py:execute_model overloads L209-227 (returns Future if non_block=True); vllm/v1/executor/multiproc_executor.py:FutureWrapper L69-100 (drains futures queue in FIFO order); WorkerProc.async_output_busy_loop L926-942 (thread-safe output copy for async scheduling).

design_decisions.decision: Executor factory pattern (Executor.get_class) to select multiproc/ray/uni based on VllmConfig.distributed_executor_backend, with unified collective_rpc interface.
design_decisions.why: Allows runtime selection of orchestration backend without changing model/scheduler code; enables testing on single GPU (UniProc) vs. multiprocess vs. Ray.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/executor/abstract.py:Executor.get_class L48-92 dispatches to MultiprocExecutor/RayDistributedExecutor/UniProcExecutor based on config; collective_rpc abstract method L199-202.

design_decisions.decision: GroupCoordinator wraps torch.distributed ProcessGroup and registers custom ops (all_reduce, all_gather, reduce_scatter) for torch.compile compatibility.
design_decisions.why: Enables compiled model forward passes (via torch.compile) that include collective ops; sidesteps torch.compile's blacklist of torch.distributed primitives.
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/distributed/parallel_state.py:direct_register_custom_op L262-287 (registers all_reduce/reduce_scatter/all_gather as vllm custom ops); GroupCoordinator.all_reduce L502-524 (dispatches via torch.ops.vllm.all_reduce if use_custom_op_call).

complexity_hotspots: MessageQueue ring buffer synchronization (shm_broadcast.py L300-550): lock-free producer-consumer with memory fences and SpinCondition ZMQ notifications; off-by-one errors in ring indices, cross-process visibility issues.

GroupCoordinator stateless vs. stateful ProcessGroup handling (parallel_state.py L140-162): different initialization and rank lookup for elastic EP (stateless groups created at runtime) vs. standard torch.distributed.
Ray compiled DAG topology for PP (ray_executor.py L387-395): pp_tp_workers 2D indexing, rank remapping after actor creation, integration with PlacementGroupSchedulingStrategy for multi-node locality.

Multi-node multiproc executor initialization (multiproc_executor.py L134-229): leader node (node_rank_within_dp==0) creates remote mq for followers, asynchronous handshake via peer_worker_response_mqs, deadlock-prone ordering (wait_until_ready calls must be synchronized).

Elastic EP coordinate store initialization (parallel_state.py L1534-1547): StatelessProcessGroup coordination via TCP store, re-ranking during scale-up/down events, group recreation without torch.distributed.new_group.
KV transfer aggregation (executor abstract.py L224-227): unique_reply_rank selects output from PP last stage, kv_output_aggregator reconstructs distributed KV across DP ranks post-execution.

maps.one_liner: Configuration and assembly system that transforms EngineArgs into a unified VllmConfig object, then wires it through EngineCore, Executor, and Workers to construct the async-decoupled v1 LLM inference stack.

key_files.role: Central config aggregator: VllmConfig dataclass that unifies all sub-configs (model, cache, parallel, scheduler, device, kernel, compilation) with optimization levels (O0-O3) and feature gates
Argument parser and config builder: EngineArgs mirrors all CLI/API options, validates inputs, calls create_engine_config() to assemble VllmConfig from individual config objects
v1 LLM interface: wraps EngineCore (via EngineCoreClient), orchestrates InputProcessor and OutputProcessor, bridges legacy v0 API to async v1 architecture
Inner loop of the engine: receives VllmConfig, instantiates Executor, initializes KV caches, builds Scheduler (selecting async vs sync via config), sets up ModelRunnerOutput handling
Executor base class with get_class() factory: selects ExecutorBackend (ray/mp/uni/external_launcher) based on ParallelConfig.distributed_executor_backend, decouples orchestration from execution
Client abstraction to EngineCore: make_client() factory returns InprocClient (sync, in-process), SyncMPClient (sync, multiproc), or AsyncMPClient (async, multiproc) based on mode flags
Input transformation: consumes VllmConfig subsets (model_config, cache_config, scheduler_config), converts raw PromptType to EngineCoreRequest with task routing

key_classes.name: VllmConfig
key_classes.responsibility: Aggregate all sub-configs (ModelConfig, CacheConfig, ParallelConfig, SchedulerConfig, etc.) into single data structure; compute_hash() encodes graph-affecting config for caching/validation

key_classes.name: EngineArgs
key_classes.responsibility: CLI/API argument mirror; __post_init__() normalizes config dicts to typed objects; create_engine_config() orchestrates creation of all sub-configs (model, parallel, cache, etc.) and assembles VllmConfig

key_classes.name: LLMEngine
key_classes.responsibility: Facade for v1 async execution: accepts EngineArgs, creates VllmConfig, instantiates Executor, wires InputProcessor + OutputProcessor + EngineCoreClient for request/response flow

key_classes.name: EngineCore
key_classes.responsibility: Inner loop: reads VllmConfig, instantiates Executor (triggering KV cache profiling), selects Scheduler class via SchedulerConfig.get_scheduler_cls(), manages request scheduling and batch execution

key_classes.name: Executor
key_classes.responsibility: Abstract base with get_class() factory method; selects concrete executor (UniProcExecutor, MultiprocExecutor, RayDistributedExecutor) by reading ParallelConfig.distributed_executor_backend

design_decisions.decision: VllmConfig unifies all sub-configs (ModelConfig, CacheConfig, ParallelConfig, SchedulerConfig, DeviceConfig, KernelConfig, CompilationConfig, etc.) into a single immutable aggregate
design_decisions.why: Single source of truth for engine construction; enables consistent hashing, caching of compiled graphs, and dependency injection; avoids scattered config state in v0 that complicated feature selection
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/config/vllm.py:L273-L350 defines all fields with type hints and defaults; compute_hash() at L367 encodes graph-affecting factors; create_engine_config() at vllm/engine/arg_utils.py:L2149 assembles into one object

design_decisions.decision: Executor backend is selected at instantiation time via Executor.get_class(vllm_config) factory, reading ParallelConfig.distributed_executor_backend feature flag
design_decisions.why: Decouples orchestration strategy from core inference loop; enables distributed execution (ray/mp) vs single-process (uni) without code duplication; allows feature gates to select implementations dynamically
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/executor/abstract.py:L47-L92 implements get_class() factory with if/elif chain on distributed_executor_backend; vllm/v1/engine/core.py:L118 instantiates via executor_class(vllm_config)

design_decisions.decision: Scheduler class is selected via SchedulerConfig.get_scheduler_cls() method based on async_scheduling flag, returning Scheduler (sync) vs AsyncScheduler (async)
design_decisions.why: v1 supports both sync (for compatibility, lower latency) and async (for throughput, request pipelining) scheduling without duplicating request logic; feature flag drives implementation selection
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/config/scheduler.py:L168-L177 implements get_scheduler_cls() with if/elif on async_scheduling; vllm/v1/engine/core.py:L132 calls it; AsyncScheduler enables pipelined batch execution

design_decisions.decision: Optimization levels (O0-O3) map to compilation_config and kernel_config settings via OPTIMIZATION_LEVEL_* dicts, applying fusion passes and cudagraph modes conditionally
design_decisions.why: Trades startup time for inference performance; O0 has no compilation overhead, O1-O3 apply increasing torch.compile passes; feature selection is data-driven (not hardcoded in code paths)
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/config/vllm.py:L184-L270 defines OPTIMIZATION_LEVEL_* dicts with pass_config.fuse_* and cudagraph_mode settings; create_engine_config() applies optimization_level at L2171

design_decisions.decision: EngineCoreClient factory returns different client implementations (InprocClient, SyncMPClient, AsyncMPClient) based on multiprocess_mode and asyncio_mode flags, encapsulating communication strategy
design_decisions.why: Separates client transport (IPC/ZMQ) from request semantics; enables single LLMEngine interface to work with in-process core (v0-style) or remote core (distributed); asyncio_mode enables truly non-blocking request handling
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/engine/core_client.py:L81-L103 implements make_client() factory; vllm/v1/engine/llm_engine.py:L104-L110 calls it; DPAsyncMPClient extends AsyncMPClient for data parallelism load balancing

design_decisions.decision: KV cache initialization happens in EngineCore after Executor instantiation, updating CacheConfig in-place with profiled memory limits; per-device specs drive Scheduler.kv_cache_config
design_decisions.why: Memory profiling is device-specific and depends on model size (from ModelConfig); KV cache blocks must be allocated before scheduler starts; persistent batch enables zero-copy prefill/decode handoff
design_decisions.evidence: vllm/v1/engine/core.py:L128 calls _initialize_kv_caches(vllm_config); collector_rpc('get_kv_cache_spec') gathers per-device limits; vllm/v1/core/kv_cache_utils.py generates per-device block configs

complexity_hotspots: EngineArgs.create_engine_config() (L1622-L2177): massive method orchestrating config creation, validation, and assembly; handles multi-node DP rank inference, placement group setup, TurboQuant boundary protection, optimization level application, feature compatibility checks

Executor.get_class() (L47-L92): branching logic selecting executor backend; also supports custom executor_class passed as type[Executor], adding type validation and reflexive resolution of qualified names

VllmConfig.compute_hash() (L367-L420+): must track all graph-affecting factors (model, cache, quantization, LoRA, speculative config, compilation settings) and exclude everything else; complex to maintain as new configs added

EngineCoreClient.make_client() factory hierarchy: three levels of selection (multiprocess_mode, asyncio_mode, data_parallel_size + external_lb), returning InprocClient | SyncMPClient | AsyncMPClient | DPAsyncMPClient | DPLBAsyncMPClient; subclass tree is fragile

KV cache profiling and per-device block allocation: EngineCore must call executor.get_kv_cache_specs(), then generate per-device block configurations, feed into Scheduler, and ensure consistency across workers; multiple handshakes (initialization, profiling, handshake metadata propagation) are sequenced

request_lifecycle: [ENTRY] User calls `LLM.generate(prompts, sampling_params)` (offline) or HTTP POST hits the OpenAI server route — `vllm/entrypoints/llm.py:LLM.generate()` or `vllm/entrypoints/openai/api_server.py` → `OpenAIServingChat.create_chat_completion()` (chat_completion/serving.py).

[RENDER] The prompt is rendered/normalized into an EngineInput — `BaseRenderer.render_cmpl()/render_chat()` (vllm/renderers/base.py) tokenizes (AsyncMicrobatchTokenizer) and runs the multimodal processor. Offline path goes through `LLM._run_completion()`; server path calls `engine_client.generate(...)`.

[ASYNC FACADE] `AsyncLLM.generate(prompt, sampling_params, request_id)` (vllm/v1/engine/async_llm.py:524) is invoked; it returns an AsyncGenerator and immediately calls `AsyncLLM.add_request()` (async_llm.py:280).

[STAGE 1 — INPUT] `add_request()` calls `InputProcessor.process_inputs()` (vllm/v1/engine/input_processor.py:234) → validates SamplingParams, tokenizes, extracts/sorts multimodal MultiModalFeatureSpec, assigns request_id (external_req_id + uuid), and builds an immutable `EngineCoreRequest` (vllm/v1/engine/__init__.py).

[FAN-OUT (n>1)] If `params.n > 1`, a `ParentRequest` (vllm/v1/engine/parallel_sampling.py) fans out n child EngineCoreRequests (child id `{idx}_{parent}`, unique seeds); each child goes through `_add_request()` independently.

[STAGE 1 → DISPATCH] `AsyncLLM._add_request()` (async_llm.py:400) does two things in parallel: (a) registers per-request state + a `RequestOutputCollector` queue in the in-process `OutputProcessor`, and (b) ships the EngineCoreRequest across the process boundary via `EngineCoreClient.add_request_async()` (vllm/v1/engine/core_client.py:1058, AsyncMPClient → ZMQ DEALER + msgpack).

[IPC RECEIVE] In the separate EngineCore process, `EngineCoreProc.process_input_sockets()` (vllm/v1/engine/core.py:1320) receives ZMQ frames, decodes via MsgpackDecoder (+ TensorIpcReceiver for zero-copy multimodal tensors), and enqueues onto the input_queue.

[STAGE 2 — ENQUEUE] `EngineCoreProc.run_busy_loop()` (core.py:1164) → `_process_input_queue()` → `EngineCore.add_request()` (core.py:315) → `Scheduler.add_request()` (vllm/v1/core/sched/scheduler.py:1728) places the Request on the waiting queue.

[STAGE 2 — SCHEDULE] The busy loop calls `EngineCore.step()` (core.py:402) → `Scheduler.schedule()` (scheduler.py:352): token-budget continuous batching, RUNNING-first with preemption, prefix-cache lookup via `kv_cache_manager.get_computed_blocks()` and block allocation via `kv_cache_manager.allocate_slots()` (vllm/v1/core/kv_cache_manager.py), producing a `SchedulerOutput`.

[STAGE 2 — EXECUTE] `EngineCore.step()` calls `model_executor.execute_model(scheduler_output, non_block=True)` (vllm/v1/executor/abstract.py:210) → Executor (uni/mp/ray) broadcasts the RPC → `Worker.execute_model()` (vllm/v1/worker/gpu_worker.py) → `GPUModelRunner.execute_model()` (vllm/v1/worker/gpu_model_runner.py:3825).

[STAGE 2 — INPUT TENSORS] Inside the runner: `_update_states()` (gpu_model_runner.py:1065) reconciles the persistent InputBatch against SchedulerOutput, then `_prepare_inputs()` (gpu_model_runner.py:1787) builds token/position tensors, commits the block table, and computes slot_mapping (Triton). `CommonAttentionMetadata` → per-backend metadata via `AttentionMetadataBuilder.build()`.

[STAGE 2 — FORWARD] The transformer forward runs (vllm/model_executor/models/llama.py etc.); each `Attention` layer dispatches to its backend kernel (FlashAttention/FlashInfer/Triton, vllm/v1/attention/backends/*), reading/writing paged KV blocks. Forward result + hidden states are cached in `ExecuteModelState`; execute_model may return None (async two-phase).

[STAGE 2 — SAMPLE] `EngineCore.step()` then calls `model_executor.sample_tokens(grammar_output)` → `GPUModelRunner.sample_tokens()` (gpu_model_runner.py:4178) → `Sampler.forward()` (vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py) (penalties → logits processors → temperature → top-k/top-p → greedy/random) or `RejectionSampler` for speculative decoding; `_bookkeeping_sync()` (gpu_model_runner.py:3397) writes sampled tokens back into the persistent batch.

[STAGE 2 — UPDATE] `Scheduler.update_from_output()` (scheduler.py:1290) appends new tokens to each Request, runs stop/EOS detection, caches finalized KV blocks, frees finished requests, and emits a per-client dict of `EngineCoreOutputs`.
[IPC SEND] `EngineCoreProc.process_output_sockets()` (core.py:1466) msgpack-encodes EngineCoreOutputs and sends them over the ZMQ output socket back to the frontend process.

[STAGE 3 — RECEIVE] `AsyncMPClient.process_outputs_socket()` (core_client.py:942) decodes frames and puts EngineCoreOutputs onto an asyncio outputs_queue; `AsyncLLM._run_output_handler()` background task (async_llm.py:637) awaits `get_output_async()`.

[STAGE 3 — OUTPUT] The output_handler calls `OutputProcessor.process_outputs()` (vllm/v1/engine/output_processor.py:572, single batch loop): incremental detokenization (`IncrementalDetokenizer.update()`, detokenizer.py) with stop-string buffering, logprobs assembly (`LogprobsProcessor`, logprobs.py), and RequestOutput construction (DELTA vs cumulative, parent aggregation for n>1).
[STAGE 3 — ENQUEUE] Each assembled `RequestOutput` is pushed to its per-request `RequestOutputCollector.put()` (output_processor.py:45), with delta-merging if the producer outpaces the consumer.

[STREAM OUT] Back in `AsyncLLM.generate()` (async_llm.py:576), the consumer drains the queue via `q.get_nowait() or await q.get()` and yields each RequestOutput; on finish or client disconnect it aborts. The server formats each as an SSE ChatCompletionStreamResponse (or a final JSON), completing the round-trip to the user.

proposed_outline.part: Part I — The Big Picture: One Request, End to End

chapters.title: What vLLM v1 Is and How to Read This Book
chapters.scope: vLLM v1 mental model, why v1 differs from v0 (async-decoupled, persistent batch, torch.compile), the offline LLM vs OpenAI server surfaces, the subtract-only companion convention, and how source-reading chapters are structured (Source Trail + Theory).

chapters.title: The Lifecycle of a Request (Bird's-Eye Trace)
chapters.scope: Walk the full spine at low resolution: generate()/HTTP -> render -> InputProcessor -> EngineCore (schedule+execute+sample+update) -> OutputProcessor -> streamed tokens. Establishes the map every later chapter zooms into. Uses async_llm.py:524, core.py:402, output_processor.py:572 as anchor points.

chapters.title: From EngineArgs to VllmConfig: Assembling the Stack
chapters.scope: EngineArgs.create_engine_config() building ModelConfig/CacheConfig/ParallelConfig/SchedulerConfig/CompilationConfig into VllmConfig; optimization levels O0-O3; factory selection of Executor, Scheduler, and EngineCoreClient driven by feature flags; compute_hash().

chapters.title: AsyncLLM: The 3-Stage Decoupled Facade
chapters.scope: The crown-jewel architecture: in-process input preprocessing, out-of-process EngineCore, in-process background output handler. generate() async generator, add_request() fan to OutputProcessor + EngineCore, lazy output_handler task, chunked processing with asyncio.sleep(0). async_llm.py:280-707.

chapters.title: Stage 1 — Input Processing: Prompt to EngineCoreRequest
chapters.scope: InputProcessor.process_inputs(): tokenization via Renderer, param validation, request-id assignment, multimodal MultiModalFeatureSpec extraction/sorting, and EngineCoreRequest construction. input_processor.py:234-377.

chapters.title: Parallel Sampling Fan-Out (n>1)
chapters.scope: ParentRequest spawning n independent child EngineCoreRequests with unique ids and deterministic seed progression; why independent scheduling beats batched n; aggregation hooks set up for the output stage. parallel_sampling.py, async_llm.py:376-412.

chapters.title: The IPC Boundary: ZMQ, msgpack, and Zero-Copy Tensors
chapters.scope: EngineCoreClient hierarchy (Inproc/Sync/AsyncMP), ZMQ DEALER sockets, EngineCoreRequestType byte-tagged protocol, msgpack encode/decode, EngineCoreProc input/output socket threads, and TensorIpcSender/Receiver drain-and-buffer for multimodal tensors. core_client.py, core.py:1320-1531, tensor_ipc.py.

chapters.title: Stage 3 — Output Processing: Tokens to RequestOutput
chapters.scope: The single-loop OutputProcessor.process_outputs(), per-request RequestState, RequestOutputCollector queues with DELTA merging, stream_interval, finish handling, and parent aggregation for n>1. output_processor.py:572-687, 45-107.

chapters.title: Incremental Detokenization and Stop Strings
chapters.scope: IncrementalDetokenizer hierarchy (Fast tokenizers.DecodeStream vs Slow Python), stop-string buffer holdback, min_tokens guard, UTF-8 boundary recovery. detokenizer.py:30-340.

chapters.title: Logprobs Assembly with Byte-Fallback Correction
chapters.scope: Sample and prompt logprobs from EngineCore tensors, context-aware UTF-8 multi-byte reconstruction, cumulative logprob tracking, flat vs nested formats. logprobs.py:30-353.

proposed_outline.part: Part III — Inside EngineCore: The Busy Loop

chapters.title: EngineCore and the Busy Loop
chapters.scope: EngineCore.step() orchestration (schedule -> execute_model non_block -> grammar bitmask -> sample_tokens -> update_from_output), run_busy_loop, lifecycle (pause/resume/sleep), and where the batch queue plugs in. core.py:91-432, 1164-1205.

chapters.title: Pipeline Parallelism in the Loop: the Batch Queue
chapters.scope: step_with_batch_queue() buffering SchedulerOutput+future pairs to keep PP stages busy, deferred sampling for structured output, async_scheduling flag. core.py:443-559.

proposed_outline.part: Part IV — Scheduling and KV Cache

chapters.title: Continuous Batching with Token Budgets
chapters.scope: Token-centric scheduling (no prefill/decode phases), RUNNING-first, max_num_scheduled_tokens budget, SchedulerOutput (NewRequestData vs CachedRequestData persistent-batch protocol), FCFS vs Priority queues. scheduler.py:352-525, output.py.

chapters.title: Preemption, Waiting Queues, and Update-From-Output
chapters.scope: Preemption loop on allocation failure, dual waiting/skipped queues for head-of-line avoidance, update_from_output token append + stop detection + request lifecycle/cleanup. scheduler.py:466-846, 1290-1551.

chapters.title: Paged KV Cache: Block Pool and Prefix Caching
chapters.scope: BlockPool, KVCacheBlock, FreeKVCacheBlockQueue doubly-linked LRU, block hashing with extra keys (MM/LoRA/salt), BlockHashToBlockMap, reference counting touch/free. block_pool.py, kv_cache_utils.py.

chapters.title: Allocation and Multi-Attention Coordination
chapters.scope: KVCacheManager three-stage allocate_slots (free skipped / prefix hits + external / new + lookahead), KVCacheCoordinator (Unitary vs Hybrid), fixed-point cache-hit search for hybrid full+SWA+MLA models. kv_cache_manager.py:225-416, kv_cache_coordinator.py:487-591.

proposed_outline.part: Part V — Execution: Workers, Runner, Kernels

chapters.title: Executors and Worker Lifecycle
chapters.scope: Executor.get_class() factory (uni/mp/ray), collective_rpc, MultiprocExecutor shared-memory MessageQueues + FutureWrapper + worker monitoring, WorkerWrapperBase lazy init, GPU memory profiling before KV allocation. abstract.py, multiproc_executor.py, gpu_worker.py.

chapters.title: The Persistent Batch and _prepare_inputs
chapters.scope: InputBatch persisting across iterations (slot reuse via batch_update_builder), _update_states reconciling SchedulerOutput, _prepare_inputs (token index_select, positions, block table commit, Triton slot_mapping), CachedRequestState. gpu_model_runner.py:1065-1936, gpu_input_batch.py, block_table.py.

chapters.title: Decoupled Forward and Sampling: ExecuteModelState
chapters.scope: Two-phase execute_model() returning None + caching ExecuteModelState, sample_tokens() unpacking it, _bookkeeping_sync writing tokens back, CUDA graph dispatch. gpu_model_runner.py:378-391, 3825-4225.

chapters.title: Distributed Parallelism: Groups and Collectives
chapters.scope: GroupCoordinator (TP/PP/DP/EP), CPU(gloo)+device(NCCL) groups, all_reduce/all_gather/reduce_scatter, custom-op registration for torch.compile, P2P for PP. parallel_state.py:290-809, communication_op.py.

chapters.title: Async Communication and Data Parallelism
chapters.scope: AsyncIntermediateTensors lazy PP sync (isend/irecv), DPCoordinator wave synchronization with all-reduce consensus, DP load balancing (DPLBAsyncMPClient). gpu_worker.py:74-861, coordinator.py, core.py:1622-1863.

chapters.title: Model Definitions and Weight Loading
chapters.scope: Llama as case study: v1 (vllm_config, prefix) signature, ColumnParallel/QKVParallel linear with TP, get_rope() factory, model loader pipeline (initialize_model -> load_weights -> process_weights_after_loading), AutoWeightsLoader, stacked QKV/gate-up mapping. llama.py, model_loader/, linear.py.

chapters.title: Attention Backends and Metadata
chapters.scope: AttentionBackend abstraction + registry + selector, CommonAttentionMetadata -> backend-specific metadata (FlashAttention/FlashInfer/Triton), KV cache shape/stride (NHD vs HND), AOT scheduler for FA3, cascade and DCP. backend.py, selector.py, backends/flash_attn.py.

chapters.title: The Sampling Pipeline

flagship_topics: The async 3-stage decoupling: in-process input preprocessing + out-of-process EngineCore busy loop + in-process background output handler, bridged by per-request RequestOutputCollector queues (async_llm.py, the architectural heart of v1).

Continuous batching with token budgets and dynamic preemption: phase-free token-centric scheduling, RUNNING-first allocation, and the NewRequestData/CachedRequestData persistent-batch protocol (scheduler.py + output.py).

Paged KV cache with prefix caching: block pool, block hashing, the doubly-linked free queue, three-stage allocation, and fixed-point multi-attention cache-hit search (kv_cache_manager.py + block_pool.py + kv_cache_coordinator.py).

The persistent InputBatch and decoupled forward/sampling via ExecuteModelState: zero-realloc continuous batching on the GPU plus two-phase execute_model/sample_tokens enabling async scheduling and PP overlap (gpu_model_runner.py + gpu_input_batch.py).
The ZMQ+msgpack IPC boundary with zero-copy tensor transport: EngineCoreClient/EngineCoreProc, byte-tagged request protocol, and TensorIpcSender/Receiver (core_client.py + core.py + tensor_ipc.py).
open_questions: Depth of Stage-3 split: the outline gives detokenization (ch09) and logprobs (ch10) their own chapters inside the flagship Part II. Should these be demoted to a single combined chapter or pushed to an appendix to keep Part II tightly focused on the decoupling architecture rather than text-processing minutiae?

Placement of distributed parallelism (ch20-21): it is currently in Part V (Execution) because the runner/executor motivate it, but distributed groups are arguably foundational (L1). Decide whether to front-load a lighter 'distributed primitives' chapter early and defer DP wave-coordination/elastic-EP to the end.

Coverage breadth of attention backends (ch23): FlashAttention is treated as the canonical backend with FlashInfer/Triton as variants. Confirm whether FlashInfer (the largest file, 1958 LOC, NVIDIA cascade + TRT-LLM) and MLA deserve dedicated chapters or remain comparison sections — this materially changes Part VI length.

Spec-decode dependency on attention/scheduler (ch25): rejection sampling depends on sampling, but proposer KV/slot-mapping and tree/cross attention depend on ch23 and ch14. Verify the chosen depends_on chain does not force readers through too much execution detail before the spec-decode payoff; consider a lighter conceptual spec-decode chapter earlier with the kernels deferred.

Granularity of the model-definitions chapter (ch22): weight loading, tensor-parallel linear layers, and RoPE are bundled. For advanced readers this may warrant splitting (weight loading vs parallel layers vs position embeddings), at the cost of more chapters and a longer Part VI.

Where multimodal and LoRA live: multimodal feature handling is currently woven into ch05 (input) and ch15/16 (cache hashing) rather than a standalone Part. Decide whether multimodal + LoRA deserve a dedicated cross-cutting Part, given they touch input, kv-cache hashing, attention (encoder), and model definitions.

Sync LLMEngine vs AsyncLLM emphasis: the book centers AsyncLLM, with the sync path appearing only in ch26. Confirm this is acceptable for advanced readers, or whether the sync LLMEngine.step() loop should be introduced earlier as the simpler pedagogical entry before the async machinery.

Total length vs progression: 28 chapters across 7 Parts mirrors the existing 28-chapter outline count but the subsystem coverage differs from the published Parts 1-2. Decide whether to align chapter IDs/titles with the already-published 13 chapters or treat this as a fresh v1-source-reading outline with new unique IDs.
